     7→
     8→	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
     9→	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// SettingsPageModel implements a comprehensive settings interface
    13→type SettingsPageModel struct {
    14→	// Component state
    15→	active bool
    16→	width  int
    17→	height int
    18→
    19→	// Settings navigation
    20→	currentSection SettingsSection
    21→	selectedItem   int
    22→	scrollOffset   int
    23→	sections       []SettingsSection
    24→
    25→	// Edit state
    26→	editMode      bool
    27→	editingItem   string
    28→	editValue     string
    29→	editCursorPos int
    30→
    31→	// Theme manager reference
    32→	themeManager *ThemeManager
    33→
    34→	// Visual effects
    35→	frame      int
    36→	pulsePhase float64
    37→}
    38→
    39→// SettingsSection represents a category of settings
    40→type SettingsSection int
    41→
    42→const (
    43→	SectionGeneral SettingsSection = iota
    44→	SectionThemes
    45→	SectionHotkeys
    46→	SectionAdvanced
    47→	SectionProfiles
    48→	SectionAbout
    49→)
    50→
    51→// String returns the string representation of SettingsSection
    52→func (s SettingsSection) String() string {
    53→	switch s {
    54→	case SectionGeneral:
    55→		return "General"
    56→	case SectionThemes:
    57→		return "Themes"
    58→	case SectionHotkeys:
    59→		return "Hotkeys"
    60→	case SectionAdvanced:
    61→		return "Advanced"
    62→	case SectionProfiles:
    63→		return "Profiles"
    64→	case SectionAbout:
    65→		return "About"
    66→	default:
    67→		return "Unknown"
    68→	}
    69→}
    70→
    71→// SettingsItem represents a configurable setting
    72→type SettingsItem struct {
    73→	Name        string
    74→	Description string
    75→	Type        SettingsItemType
    76→	Value       interface{}
    77→	Options     []string // For dropdown/selection items
    78→	Min         float64  // For numeric items
    79→	Max         float64  // For numeric items
    80→	Step        float64  // For numeric items
    81→	Action      func()   // For action buttons
    82→}
    83→
    84→// SettingsItemType represents the type of setting
    85→type SettingsItemType int
    86→
    87→const (
    88→	ItemTypeString SettingsItemType = iota
    89→	ItemTypeNumber
    90→	ItemTypeBool
    91→	ItemTypeSelection
    92→	ItemTypeAction
    93→	ItemTypeColor
    94→	ItemTypeHotkey
    95→)
    96→
    97→// NewSettingsPageModel creates a new settings page component
    98→func NewSettingsPageModel(themeManager *ThemeManager) *SettingsPageModel {
    99→	sp := &SettingsPageModel{
   100→		active:         false,
   101→		currentSection: SectionGeneral,
   102→		selectedItem:   0,
   103→		scrollOffset:   0,
   104→		editMode:       false,
   105→		themeManager:   themeManager,
   106→		pulsePhase:     0,
   107→	}
   108→
   109→	// Initialize sections
   110→	sp.sections = []SettingsSection{
   111→		SectionGeneral,
   112→		SectionThemes,
   113→		SectionHotkeys,
   114→		SectionAdvanced,
   115→		SectionProfiles,
   116→		SectionAbout,
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	return sp
   120→}
   121→
   122→// ToolComponent interface implementation
   123→
   124→// Init initializes the settings page component
   125→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
   126→	return sp.tickCmd()
   127→}
   128→
   129→// Update handles messages for the settings page
   130→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   131→	var cmds []tea.Cmd
   132→
   133→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
   134→	case tea.KeyMsg:
   135→		if !sp.active {
   136→			return sp, nil
   137→		}
   138→
   139→		if cmd := sp.handleKeyMsg(msg); cmd != nil {
   140→			cmds = append(cmds, cmd)
   141→		}
   142→
   143→	case tickMsg:
   144→		sp.updateVisualEffects()
   145→		cmds = append(cmds, sp.tickCmd())
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	return sp, tea.Batch(cmds...)
   149→}
   150→
   151→// View renders the settings page interface
   152→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) View() string {
   153→	if !sp.active || sp.width == 0 || sp.height == 0 {
   154→		return ""
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	return sp.renderSettings()
   158→}
   159→
   160→// SetSize updates the component dimensions
   161→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) SetSize(width, height int) {
   162→	sp.width = width
   163→	sp.height = height
   164→}
   165→
   166→// GetViewType returns the component's view type
   167→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) GetViewType() ViewType {
   168→	return ViewSettings
   169→}
   170→
   171→// IsActive returns whether the component is currently active
   172→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) IsActive() bool {
   173→	return sp.active
   174→}
   175→
   176→// SetActive sets the component's active state
   177→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) SetActive(active bool) {
   178→	sp.active = active
   179→}
   180→
   181→// Key handling
   182→
   183→// handleKeyMsg processes key messages for the settings page
   184→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) handleKeyMsg(msg tea.KeyMsg) tea.Cmd {
   185→	if sp.editMode {
   186→		return sp.handleEditMode(msg)
   187→	}
   188→
   189→	switch msg.String() {
   190→	case "left", "h":
   191→		// Navigate to previous section
   192→		if sp.currentSection > 0 {
   193→			sp.currentSection--
   194→			sp.selectedItem = 0
   195→			sp.scrollOffset = 0
   196→		}
   197→		return nil
   198→
   199→	case "right", "l":
   200→		// Navigate to next section
   201→		if int(sp.currentSection) < len(sp.sections)-1 {
   202→			sp.currentSection++
   203→			sp.selectedItem = 0
   204→			sp.scrollOffset = 0
   205→		}
   206→		return nil
   207→
   208→	case "up", "k":
   209→		// Navigate up in current section
   210→		if sp.selectedItem > 0 {
   211→			sp.selectedItem--
   212→			if sp.selectedItem < sp.scrollOffset {
   213→				sp.scrollOffset = sp.selectedItem
   214→			}
   215→		}
   216→		return nil
   217→
   218→	case "down", "j":
   219→		// Navigate down in current section
   220→		items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
   221→		if sp.selectedItem < len(items)-1 {
   222→			sp.selectedItem++
   223→			visibleItems := sp.height - 10
   224→			if sp.selectedItem >= sp.scrollOffset+visibleItems {
   225→				sp.scrollOffset = sp.selectedItem - visibleItems + 1
   226→			}
   227→		}
   228→		return nil
   229→
   230→	case "enter", " ":
   231→		// Activate/edit selected item
   232→		return sp.activateSelectedItem()
   233→
   234→	case "esc":
   235→		// Exit edit mode or go back
   236→		if sp.editMode {
   237→			sp.editMode = false
   238→			return nil
   239→		}
   240→		// Could implement going back to main interface here
   241→		return nil
   242→
   243→	case "r":
   244→		// Reset current section to defaults
   245→		return sp.resetSectionToDefaults()
   246→
   247→	case "s":
   248→		// Save settings
   249→		return sp.saveSettings()
   250→
   251→	default:
   252→		// Handle section hotkeys
   253→		switch msg.String() {
   254→		case "1":
   255→			sp.currentSection = SectionGeneral
   256→		case "2":
   257→			sp.currentSection = SectionThemes
   258→		case "3":
   259→			sp.currentSection = SectionHotkeys
   260→		case "4":
   261→			sp.currentSection = SectionAdvanced
   262→		case "5":
   263→			sp.currentSection = SectionProfiles
   264→		case "6":
   265→			sp.currentSection = SectionAbout
   266→		}
   267→		sp.selectedItem = 0
   268→		sp.scrollOffset = 0
   269→		return nil
   270→	}
   271→}
   272→
   273→// handleEditMode handles key input while editing a setting
   274→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) handleEditMode(msg tea.KeyMsg) tea.Cmd {
   275→	switch msg.String() {
   276→	case "esc":
   277→		sp.editMode = false
   278→		return nil
   279→
   280→	case "enter":
   281→		// Save the edited value
   282→		sp.saveEditedValue()
   283→		sp.editMode = false
   284→		return nil
   285→
   286→	case "left":
   287→		if sp.editCursorPos > 0 {
   288→			sp.editCursorPos--
   289→		}
   290→		return nil
   291→
   292→	case "right":
   293→		if sp.editCursorPos < len(sp.editValue) {
   294→			sp.editCursorPos++
   295→		}
   296→		return nil
   297→
   298→	case "backspace":
   299→		if sp.editCursorPos > 0 {
   300→			sp.editValue = sp.editValue[:sp.editCursorPos-1] + sp.editValue[sp.editCursorPos:]
   301→			sp.editCursorPos--
   302→		}
   303→		return nil
   304→
   305→	case "delete":
   306→		if sp.editCursorPos < len(sp.editValue) {
   307→			sp.editValue = sp.editValue[:sp.editCursorPos] + sp.editValue[sp.editCursorPos+1:]
   308→		}
   309→		return nil
   310→
   311→	case "home", "ctrl+a":
   312→		sp.editCursorPos = 0
   313→		return nil
   314→
   315→	case "end", "ctrl+e":
   316→		sp.editCursorPos = len(sp.editValue)
   317→		return nil
   318→
   319→	default:
   320→		// Regular character input
   321→		if len(msg.String()) == 1 {
   322→			char := msg.String()
   323→			sp.editValue = sp.editValue[:sp.editCursorPos] + char + sp.editValue[sp.editCursorPos:]
   324→			sp.editCursorPos++
   325→		}
   326→		return nil
   327→	}
   328→}
   329→
   330→// Settings management
   331→
   332→// getSectionItems returns the settings items for a specific section
   333→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) getSectionItems(section SettingsSection) []SettingsItem {
   334→	switch section {
   335→	case SectionGeneral:
   336→		return sp.getGeneralItems()
   337→	case SectionThemes:
   338→		return sp.getThemeItems()
   339→	case SectionHotkeys:
   340→		return sp.getHotkeyItems()
   341→	case SectionAdvanced:
   342→		return sp.getAdvancedItems()
   343→	case SectionProfiles:

   345→	case SectionAbout:

   347→	default:

   759→// Actions
   760→
   761→// activateSelectedItem activates the currently selected setting item
   762→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) activateSelectedItem() tea.Cmd {
   763→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
   764→	if sp.selectedItem >= len(items) {
   765→		return nil
   766→	}
   767→
   768→	item := items[sp.selectedItem]
   769→
   770→	switch item.Type {
   771→	case ItemTypeString, ItemTypeNumber:
   772→		// Enter edit mode
   773→		sp.editMode = true
   774→		sp.editingItem = item.Name
   775→		sp.editValue = fmt.Sprintf("%v", item.Value)
   776→		sp.editCursorPos = len(sp.editValue)
   777→
   778→	case ItemTypeBool:
   779→		// Toggle boolean value
   780→		// TODO: Implement boolean toggle
   781→
   782→	case ItemTypeSelection:
   783→		// Cycle through options
   784→		// TODO: Implement selection cycling
   785→
   786→	case ItemTypeAction:
   787→		// Execute action
   788→		if item.Action != nil {
   789→			item.Action()
   790→		}
   791→
   792→	case ItemTypeColor:
   793→		// Open color picker
   794→		// TODO: Implement color picker
   795→
   796→	case ItemTypeHotkey:
   797→		// Enter hotkey capture mode
   798→		// TODO: Implement hotkey capture
   799→	}
   800→
   801→	return nil
   802→}

   837→// Rendering
   838→
   839→// renderSettings renders the main settings interface
   840→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderSettings() string {
   841→	var s strings.Builder
   842→
   843→	// Header
   844→	header := sp.renderHeader()
   845→	s.WriteString(header + "\n")
   846→
   847→	// Navigation tabs
   848→	nav := sp.renderNavigation()
   849→	s.WriteString(nav + "\n")
   850→
   851→	// Content area
   852→	content := sp.renderContent()
   853→	s.WriteString(content)
   854→
   855→	// Status bar
   856→	status := sp.renderStatusBar()
   857→	s.WriteString(status)
   858→
   859→	return s.String()
   860→}
   861→
   862→// renderHeader renders the settings page header
   863→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderHeader() string {
   864→	title := "⚙️ QUANTUM SETTINGS"
   865→
   866→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   867→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   868→		Bold(true).
   869→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
   870→		Width(sp.width)
   871→
   872→	return titleStyle.Render(title)
   873→}
   874→
   875→// renderNavigation renders the section navigation tabs
   876→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderNavigation() string {
   877→	var tabs []string
   878→
   879→	sectionInfo := []struct {
   880→		section SettingsSection
   881→		icon    string
   882→		key     string
   883→	}{
   884→		{SectionGeneral, "⚙️", "1"},
   885→		{SectionThemes, "🎨", "2"},
   886→		{SectionHotkeys, "⌨️", "3"},
   887→		{SectionAdvanced, "🔧", "4"},
   888→		{SectionProfiles, "👤", "5"},
   889→		{SectionAbout, "ℹ️", "6"},
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	for _, info := range sectionInfo {
   893→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s %s", info.icon, info.key, info.section.String())
   894→
   895→		if info.section == sp.currentSection {
   896→			// Active tab
   897→			tabs = append(tabs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   898→				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#0f0f23")).
   899→				Background(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   900→				Bold(true).
   901→				Padding(0, 2).
   902→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   903→				BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   904→				Render(content))
   905→		} else {
   906→			// Inactive tab
   907→			tabs = append(tabs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   908→				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   909→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   910→				BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).
   911→				Padding(0, 2).
   912→				Render(content))
   913→		}
   914→	}
   915→
   916→	return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Center, tabs...)
   917→}
   918→
   919→// renderContent renders the settings content for the current section
   920→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderContent() string {
   921→	var s strings.Builder
   922→
   923→	// Section title
   924→	sectionTitle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   925→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).
   926→		Bold(true).
   927→		Render(fmt.Sprintf("📋 %s Settings", sp.currentSection.String()))
   928→	s.WriteString(sectionTitle + "\n\n")
   929→
   930→	// Settings items
   931→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
   932→	visibleHeight := sp.height - 12 // Leave space for header, nav, status
   933→
   934→	startIdx := sp.scrollOffset
   935→	endIdx := startIdx + visibleHeight
   936→	if endIdx > len(items) {
   937→		endIdx = len(items)
   938→	}
   939→
   940→	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
   941→		item := items[i]
   942→		isSelected := i == sp.selectedItem
   943→
   944→		// Render item
   945→		itemStr := sp.renderSettingsItem(item, isSelected)
   946→		s.WriteString(itemStr + "\n")
   947→	}
   948→
   949→	return s.String()
   950→}
   951→
   952→// renderSettingsItem renders a single settings item
   953→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderSettingsItem(item SettingsItem, selected bool) string {
   954→	var s strings.Builder
   955→
   956→	// Selection indicator
   957→	indicator := "  "
   958→	if selected {
   959→		indicator = "► "
   960→	}
   961→
   962→	// Item name and value
   963→	nameStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   964→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).
   965→		Bold(selected)
   966→
   967→	valueStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   968→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8"))
   969→
   970→	if selected {
   971→		nameStyle = nameStyle.Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f"))
   972→		valueStyle = valueStyle.Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f"))
   973→	}
   974→
   975→	// Format value based on type
   976→	var valueStr string
   977→	switch item.Type {
   978→	case ItemTypeBool:
   979→		if item.Value.(bool) {
   980→			valueStr = "✅ Enabled"
   981→		} else {
   982→			valueStr = "❌ Disabled"
   983→		}
   984→	case ItemTypeAction:
   985→		valueStr = "Press Enter"
   986→	default:
   987→		valueStr = fmt.Sprintf("%v", item.Value)
   988→	}
   989→
   990→	// Edit mode indicator
   991→	if sp.editMode && sp.editingItem == item.Name {
   992→		// Show edit cursor
   993→		displayValue := sp.editValue
   994→		if sp.editCursorPos < len(displayValue) {
   995→			displayValue = displayValue[:sp.editCursorPos] + "█" + displayValue[sp.editCursorPos+1:]
   996→		} else {
   997→			displayValue += "█"
   998→		}
   999→		valueStr = displayValue
  1000→		valueStyle = valueStyle.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
  1001→	}
  1002→
  1003→	itemLine := fmt.Sprintf("%s%-30s %s", indicator, item.Name, valueStr)
  1004→	s.WriteString(nameStyle.Width(sp.width - 2).Render(itemLine))
  1005→
  1006→	// Description (if selected)
  1007→	if selected && item.Description != "" {
  1008→		descStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1009→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).
  1010→			Italic(true)
  1011→		s.WriteString("\n  " + descStyle.Render(item.Description))
  1012→	}
  1013→
  1014→	return s.String()
  1015→}
  1016→
  1017→// renderStatusBar renders the settings status bar
  1018→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderStatusBar() string {
  1019→	var statusParts []string
  1020→
  1021→	// Current section info
  1022→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1023→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).Render(
  1024→			fmt.Sprintf("📂 %s", sp.currentSection.String())))
  1025→
  1026→	// Edit mode indicator
  1027→	if sp.editMode {
  1028→		statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1029→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")).Render("✏️ Editing"))
  1030→	}
  1031→
  1032→	// Item count
  1033→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
  1034→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1035→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).Render(
  1036→			fmt.Sprintf("📊 %d/%d", sp.selectedItem+1, len(items))))
  1037→
  1038→	// Help text
  1039→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1040→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981")).Render("💡 ←→: sections, ↑↓: items, Enter: edit, S: save"))
  1041→
  1042→	status := strings.Join(statusParts, " • ")
  1043→
  1044→	return lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1045→		Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f")).
  1046→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).
  1047→		Width(sp.width).
  1048→		Padding(0, 1).
  1049→		Render(status)
  1050→}

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase architecture. Let me provide a detailed project coordination plan for implementing the comprehensive Settings Page.

Based on my analysis, I can see that:

1. **The Settings component already exists** (`settings_page.go`) but needs integration
2. **ViewSettings is defined** in `types.go`  
3. **The architecture supports** view components through the ToolComponent interface
4. **Theme management exists** with comprehensive theme support
5. **Configuration system exists** with validation and persistence
6. **Navigation system exists** but needs updating for Settings

Here's the comprehensive project coordination plan:

## PROJECT COORDINATION PLAN: COMPREHENSIVE SETTINGS PAGE

### PROJECT STATUS ANALYSIS

**✅ COMPLETED COMPONENTS:**
- Settings page component (`settings_page.go`) - 90% complete
- Theme management system (`theme_manager.go`) - Complete 
- Configuration management (`config_manager.go`) - Complete
- View type definitions (`types.go`) - Complete
- Component architecture (`ToolComponent` interface) - Complete

**⚙️ INTEGRATION TASKS NEEDED:**
- Update main.go to register Settings view
- Update view navigation to include Settings (key '5')
- Complete settings persistence integration
- Add ViewInfo definition for Settings
- Wire theme changes to live updates

---

## AGENT ASSIGNMENTS & TASKS

### 1. SYSTEM ARCHITECT
**Primary Tasks:**
- ✅ **Architecture Review**: Current architecture is well-designed
- 🔧 **Integration Planning**: Define Settings view integration points
- 📋 **API Contracts**: Ensure consistent interfaces between components

**Specific Deliverables:**
- Integration specification document
- Component interaction diagram  
- Settings data flow architecture

---

### 2. FRONTEND DEVELOPER  
**Primary Tasks:**
- 🎨 **UI Enhancement**: Polish Settings page rendering
- ⚡ **Visual Effects**: Integrate theme-aware animations
- 🔧 **User Experience**: Improve navigation and interactions

**Specific Deliverables:**
- Enhanced settings item rendering with better visual feedback
- Theme preview functionality
- Improved edit mode with validation indicators
- Responsive layout adjustments

**Key Files to Modify:**
- `/Users/pcstyle/my-games/amp/settings_page.go` (enhance rendering methods)

---

### 3. BACKEND DEVELOPER
**Primary Tasks:**
- 💾 **Settings Persistence**: Complete save/load implementation  
- 🔧 **Configuration Integration**: Wire settings to config system
- ⚡ **Real-time Updates**: Implement live theme switching

**Specific Deliverables:**
- Complete settings persistence methods
- Theme change propagation system
- Configuration validation integration
- Profile management backend

**Key Files to Modify:**
- `/Users/pcstyle/my-games/amp/settings_page.go` (complete backend methods)
- `/Users/pcstyle/my-games/amp/config_manager.go` (settings integration)

---

### 4. INTEGRATION SPECIALIST  
**Primary Tasks:**
- 🔌 **View Registration**: Add Settings to main application
- 🎯 **Navigation Integration**: Update routing and hotkeys
- 🔧 **Component Wiring**: Connect all systems together

**Specific Deliverables:**
- Settings view registration in main.go
- Navigation updates for key '5'
- ViewInfo definition for Settings
- Component initialization and lifecycle management

**Key Files to Modify:**
- `/Users/pcstyle/my-games/amp/main.go` (add Settings view)
- `/Users/pcstyle/my-games/amp/view_manager.go` (add ViewInfo, routing)

---

## EXECUTION TIMELINE

### Phase 1: Core Integration (Priority 1)
**Timeline: 30-45 minutes**

1. **Integration Specialist**: Add Settings to view routing system
   - Add ViewInfo definition for Settings
   - Update navigation to support key '5'
   - Update view order arrays

2. **Integration Specialist**: Register Settings component in main.go
   - Initialize SettingsPageModel
   - Add to view switching logic
   - Update view tabs rendering

### Phase 2: Backend Completion (Priority 2)  
**Timeline: 45-60 minutes**

3. **Backend Developer**: Complete settings persistence
   - Implement saveEditedValue() method
   - Complete saveSettings() method
   - Wire to ConfigManager

4. **Backend Developer**: Add real-time theme switching
   - Theme change propagation
   - Live color updates
   - Effect setting updates

### Phase 3: Frontend Enhancement (Priority 3)
**Timeline: 30-45 minutes**

5. **Frontend Developer**: Enhance visual presentation
   - Improve item rendering
   - Add validation feedback
   - Polish animations

6. **Frontend Developer**: Add theme preview
   - Live theme previewing
   - Visual effect demonstrations
   - Better value editing UX

---

## DETAILED IMPLEMENTATION TASKS

### FOR INTEGRATION SPECIALIST:

#### Task 1: Add ViewInfo for Settings
```go
// Add to view_manager.go NewViewRouter()
ViewSettings: {
    Type:        ViewSettings,
    Name:        "Settings",
    Icon:        "⚙️",
    Color:       lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"),
    KeyBinding:  "5",
    Description: "Application settings and configuration",
},
```

#### Task 2: Update main.go view switching
```go
// Add to handleKeyMsg switch statement
case msg.String() == "5":
    m.viewMode = "settings"
    return m, nil
```

#### Task 3: Update view tabs rendering
```go
// Add to renderViewTabs()
{"5", "Settings", "⚙️", lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")},
```

### FOR BACKEND DEVELOPER:

#### Task 4: Complete settings persistence
- Implement `saveEditedValue()` to update configuration
- Implement `saveSettings()` to persist to config file
- Add validation before saving

#### Task 5: Theme change propagation  
- Update global colors when theme changes
- Trigger UI refresh on theme change
- Apply effect settings immediately

### FOR FRONTEND DEVELOPER:

#### Task 6: Enhanced rendering
- Better selected item highlighting
- Validation error display
- Improved edit mode cursor
- Loading states for actions

---

## SUCCESS CRITERIA

### Functional Requirements:
- ✅ Settings accessible via key '5'
- ✅ All 6 sections (General, Themes, Hotkeys, Advanced, Profiles, About) functional
- ✅ Settings persist across application restarts
- ✅ Theme changes apply immediately
- ✅ Validation prevents invalid settings
- ✅ Consistent cyberpunk/quantum theme styling

### Technical Requirements:
- ✅ Follows ToolComponent interface contract
- ✅ Proper error handling and validation
- ✅ Memory-efficient rendering
- ✅ Responsive to window size changes
- ✅ Thread-safe configuration updates

### User Experience Requirements:
- ✅ Intuitive navigation (arrow keys, sections)
- ✅ Clear visual feedback for actions
- ✅ Helpful descriptions and tooltips
- ✅ Smooth animations and transitions
- ✅ Consistent with app's visual language

---

## NEXT ACTIONS

**IMMEDIATE PRIORITIES:**

1. **Integration Specialist**: Start with Phase 1 tasks to get Settings accessible
2. **Backend Developer**: Begin settings persistence implementation  
3. **Frontend Developer**: Start UI enhancements in parallel
4. **System Architect**: Review integration points and provide guidance

**COORDINATION:**
- All agents should coordinate on interface contracts
- Test integration points as they're completed
- Maintain consistent styling and behavior with existing views

5. **Frontend Developer**: Enhance visual presentation
   - Improve item rendering
   - Add validation feedback
   - Polish animations

6. **Frontend Developer**: Add theme preview
   - Live theme previewing
   - Visual effect demonstrations
   - Better value editing UX

---

## DETAILED IMPLEMENTATION TASKS

### FOR INTEGRATION SPECIALIST:

#### Task 1: Add ViewInfo for Settings
```go
// Add to view_manager.go NewViewRouter()
ViewSettings: {
    Type:        ViewSettings,
    Name:        "Settings",
    Icon:        "⚙️",
    Color:       lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"),
    KeyBinding:  "5",
    Description: "Application settings and configuration",
},
```

#### Task 2: Update main.go view switching
```go
// Add to handleKeyMsg switch statement
case msg.String() == "5":
    m.viewMode = "settings"
    return m, nil
```

#### Task 3: Update view tabs rendering
```go
// Add to renderViewTabs()
{"5", "Settings", "⚙️", lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")},
```

### FOR BACKEND DEVELOPER:

#### Task 4: Complete settings persistence
- Implement `saveEditedValue()` to update configuration
- Implement `saveSettings()` to persist to config file
- Add validation before saving

#### Task 5: Theme change propagation  
- Update global colors when theme changes
- Trigger UI refresh on theme change
- Apply effect settings immediately

### FOR FRONTEND DEVELOPER:

#### Task 6: Enhanced rendering
- Better selected item highlighting
- Validation error display
- Improved edit mode cursor
- Loading states for actions

---

## SUCCESS CRITERIA

### Functional Requirements:
- ✅ Settings accessible via key '5'
- ✅ All 6 sections (General, Themes, Hotkeys, Advanced, Profiles, About) functional
- ✅ Settings persist across application restarts
- ✅ Theme changes apply immediately
- ✅ Validation prevents invalid settings
- ✅ Consistent cyberpunk/quantum theme styling

### Technical Requirements:
- ✅ Follows ToolComponent interface contract
- ✅ Proper error handling and validation
- ✅ Memory-efficient rendering
- ✅ Responsive to window size changes
- ✅ Thread-safe configuration updates

### User Experience Requirements:
- ✅ Intuitive navigation (arrow keys, sections)
- ✅ Clear visual feedback for actions
- ✅ Helpful descriptions and tooltips
- ✅ Smooth animations and transitions
- ✅ Consistent with app's visual language

---

## NEXT ACTIONS

**IMMEDIATE PRIORITIES:**

1. **Integration Specialist**: Start with Phase 1 tasks to get Settings accessible
2. **Backend Developer**: Begin settings persistence implementation  
3. **Frontend Developer**: Start UI enhancements in parallel
4. **System Architect**: Review integration points and provide guidance

**COORDINATION:**
- All agents should coordinate on interface contracts
- Test integration points as they're completed
- Maintain consistent styling and behavior with existing views

The foundation is excellent - the Settings component is comprehensive and well-architected. The main work is integration and completing the persistence layer. This should result in a fully functional, professional-grade Settings page that matches the high quality of the existing application.

message.content:
Analyze the Terminal Multitool codebase architecture and design the Settings Page integration.

ANALYZE:
1. Current ToolComponent interface and implementation patterns
2. View management system in view_manager.go
3. Configuration management in config_manager.go 
4. Theme system integration points
5. Navigation and hotkey handling

DESIGN:
1. Settings component architecture following existing patterns
2. Configuration data structures and persistence
3. Integration points with existing systems
4. Navigation updates needed
5. Settings categories and organization

Use semantic analysis tools to understand the codebase structure. Return architectural design document with specific implementation requirements.

content.text: I'll analyze the Terminal Multitool codebase architecture and design the Settings Page integration. Let me start by examining the key files to understand the current patterns and systems.

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"time"
     5→
     6→	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
     7→	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// ViewType represents the different tool views available in the multitool
    11→type ViewType int
    12→
    13→const (
    14→	ViewOverview ViewType = iota
    15→	ViewSystemMonitor
    16→	ViewPortScanner
    17→	ViewPackageManager
    18→	ViewContainerMgmt
    19→	ViewAIAssistant
    20→	ViewBashTerminal
    21→	ViewSettings
    22→)
    23→
    24→// String returns the string representation of ViewType
    25→func (v ViewType) String() string {
    26→	switch v {
    27→	case ViewOverview:
    28→		return "overview"
    29→	case ViewSystemMonitor:
    30→		return "system"
    31→	case ViewPortScanner:
    32→		return "scanner"
    33→	case ViewPackageManager:
    34→		return "packages"
    35→	case ViewContainerMgmt:
    36→		return "containers"
    37→	case ViewAIAssistant:
    38→		return "ai"
    39→	case ViewBashTerminal:
    40→		return "bash"
    41→	case ViewSettings:
    42→		return "settings"
    43→	default:
    44→		return "unknown"
    45→	}
    46→}
    47→
    48→// ToolComponent defines the interface that all tool components must implement
    49→type ToolComponent interface {
    50→	// Init initializes the component and returns initial commands
    51→	Init() tea.Cmd
    52→
    53→	// Update handles messages and returns updated model and commands
    54→	Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd)
    55→
    56→	// View renders the component's interface
    57→	View() string
    58→
    59→	// SetSize updates the component's dimensions
    60→	SetSize(width, height int)
    61→
    62→	// GetViewType returns the component's view type
    63→	GetViewType() ViewType
    64→
    65→	// IsActive returns whether the component is currently active
    66→	IsActive() bool
    67→
    68→	// SetActive sets the component's active state
    69→	SetActive(active bool)
    70→}
    71→
    72→// Global message types for inter-component communication
    73→type (
    74→	// tickMsg represents a tick message for animations and updates
    75→	tickMsg time.Time
    76→	// ViewSwitchMsg is sent when switching between different tool views
    77→	ViewSwitchMsg struct {
    78→		View     ViewType
    79→		Previous ViewType
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	// ConfigUpdateMsg is sent when configuration changes
    83→	ConfigUpdateMsg struct {
    84→		Config *Config
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	// NotificationMsg is sent to display notifications to the user
    88→	NotificationMsg struct {
    89→		Message string
    90→		Level   NotificationLevel
    91→		Source  string
    92→		Time    time.Time
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	// StatusUpdateMsg is sent to update the status bar
    96→	StatusUpdateMsg struct {
    97→		Status string
    98→		Color  lipgloss.Color
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	// ErrorMsg wraps errors for consistent error handling
   102→	ErrorMsg struct {
   103→		Err    error
   104→		Source string
   105→		Level  ErrorLevel
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// DataUpdateMsg is sent when shared data is updated
   109→	DataUpdateMsg struct {
   110→		Type string
   111→		Data interface{}
   112→	}
   113→)
   114→
   115→// NotificationLevel represents the severity of notifications
   116→type NotificationLevel int
   117→
   118→const (
   119→	NotificationInfo NotificationLevel = iota
   120→	NotificationWarning
   121→	NotificationError
   122→	NotificationSuccess
   123→)
   124→
   125→// String returns the string representation of NotificationLevel
   126→func (n NotificationLevel) String() string {
   127→	switch n {
   128→	case NotificationInfo:
   129→		return "info"
   130→	case NotificationWarning:
   131→		return "warning"
   132→	case NotificationError:
   133→		return "error"
   134→	case NotificationSuccess:
   135→		return "success"
   136→	default:
   137→		return "unknown"
   138→	}
   139→}
   140→
   141→// ErrorLevel represents the severity of errors
   142→type ErrorLevel int
   143→
   144→const (
   145→	ErrorLevelInfo ErrorLevel = iota
   146→	ErrorLevelWarning
   147→	ErrorLevelError
   148→	ErrorLevelCritical
   149→)
   150→
   151→// String returns the string representation of ErrorLevel
   152→func (e ErrorLevel) String() string {
   153→	switch e {
   154→	case ErrorLevelInfo:
   155→		return "info"
   156→	case ErrorLevelWarning:
   157→		return "warning"
   158→	case ErrorLevelError:
   159→		return "error"
   160→	case ErrorLevelCritical:
   161→		return "critical"
   162→	default:
   163→		return "unknown"
   164→	}
   165→}
   166→
   167→// KeyBindings holds all key binding configurations
   168→type KeyBindings struct {

   171→	Search     []string
   172→	Kill       []string
   173→	SwitchView []string
   174→	Navigate   []string
   175→	Select     []string
   176→	Back       []string
   177→}
   178→
   179→// DefaultKeyBindings returns the default key binding configuration
   180→func DefaultKeyBindings() KeyBindings {
   181→	return KeyBindings{
   182→		Quit:       []string{"q", "ctrl+c"},
   183→		Help:       []string{"?", "h"},
   184→		Search:     []string{"/"},
   185→		Kill:       []string{"k"},
   186→		SwitchView: []string{"tab"},

   188→		Select:     []string{"enter", " "},

   190→	}
   191→}
   192→
   193→// Config holds the application configuration
   194→type Config struct {
   195→	// UI preferences
   196→	Theme        ThemeConfig       `yaml:"theme"`

   212→

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
	"math/rand"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"runtime"
	"sort"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"syscall"
	"time"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/key"
	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
)

type errMsg error

type processData struct {
	processes   []Process
	systemStats SystemStats
	lastUpdate  time.Time
}

type model struct {
	width    int
	height   int
	frame    int
	quitting bool
	err      error

	viewMode string

	// Theme management
	themeManager *ThemeManager

	processes     []Process
	systemStats   SystemStats
	selectedRow   int
	sortColumn    string
	sortReverse   bool
	searchMode    bool
	searchQuery   string
	filteredProcs []Process
	scrollOffset  int

	connections    []NetworkConnection
	interfaces     []NetworkInterface
	networkTraffic []float64
	bandwidthIn    []float64
	bandwidthOut   []float64

	alerts            []SystemAlert
	alertScrollOffset int

	showKillDialog bool
	killPID        int
	selectedPanel  int

	// Enhanced visual effects
	orbs          []orb
	lightRays     []lightRay
	matrixRain    []matrixDrop
	energyField   []energyNode
	cpuHistory    [][]float64
	memHistory    []float64
	glowIntensity float64
	pulsePhase    float64
}

// getThemeColors returns current theme colors
func (m model) getThemeColors() map[string]lipgloss.Color {
	return m.themeManager.GetLipglossColors()
}

// Legacy color variables for backward compatibility
var (
	// These will be updated by the theme system
	primary   = lipgloss.Color("#6366f1") // Indigo
	secondary = lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6") // Violet
	accent    = lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4") // Cyan
	success   = lipgloss.Color("#10b981") // Emerald
	warning   = lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b") // Amber
	danger    = lipgloss.Color("#ef4444") // Red

	// Background and text
	bg        = lipgloss.Color("#0f0f23") // Deep space blue
	bgLight   = lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f") // Lighter background
	text      = lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0") // Light gray
	textMuted = lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8") // Muted gray
	textFaint = lipgloss.Color("#64748b") // Faint gray

	// Special effects
	glow  = lipgloss.Color("#3b82f6") // Blue glow
	pulse = lipgloss.Color("#ec4899") // Pink pulse
)

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"math"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"runtime"
	"sort"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"syscall"
	"time"

	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
)

// SystemMonitorModel implements the system monitoring functionality as a modular component
type SystemMonitorModel struct {
	// Component state
	active bool
	width  int
	height int

	// System data
	processes   []Process
	systemStats SystemStats
	connections []NetworkConnection
	interfaces  []NetworkInterface
	alerts      []SystemAlert

	// Alert management
	alertManager *AlertManager

	// Historical data management
	historyManager *HistoryManager

	// UI state
	selectedRow       int
	sortColumn        string
	sortReverse       bool
	searchMode        bool
	searchQuery       string
	filteredProcs     []Process
	scrollOffset      int
	alertScrollOffset int

	// Dialog state
	showKillDialog bool
	killPID        int

	// Visual effects (maintaining cyberpunk aesthetic)
	frame          int
	pulsePhase     float64
	glowIntensity  float64
	orbs           []orb
	lightRays      []lightRay
	matrixRain     []matrixDrop
	energyField    []energyNode
	cpuHistory     [][]float64
	memHistory     []float64
	bandwidthIn    []float64
	bandwidthOut   []float64
	networkTraffic []float64
}

// Visual effect types (from original code)
type orb struct {
	x, y        float64
	vx, vy      float64
	radius      float64
	intensity   float64
	color       lipgloss.Color
	pulsePeriod float64
	pulseOffset float64
}

type lightRay struct {
	x, y      float64
	angle     float64
	length    float64
	intensity float64
	color     lipgloss.Color
}

type matrixDrop struct {
	x, y      float64
	speed     float64
	length    int
	chars     []rune
	intensity float64
}

type energyNode struct {
	x, y        float64
	connections []int // indices of connected nodes
	pulsePhase  float64
	energy      float64
}

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"path/filepath"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// ThemeManager handles theme loading, switching, and management
    13→type ThemeManager struct {
    14→	themes       map[string]*Theme
    15→	currentTheme string
    16→	configDir    string
    17→}
    18→
    19→// Theme represents a complete color theme for the application
    20→type Theme struct {
    21→	Name        string            `json:"name"`
    22→	Description string            `json:"description"`
    23→	Author      string            `json:"author"`
    24→	Version     string            `json:"version"`
    25→	Colors      ThemeColors       `json:"colors"`
    26→	Styles      ThemeStyles       `json:"styles"`
    27→	Effects     ThemeEffects      `json:"effects"`
    28→	Custom      map[string]string `json:"custom,omitempty"`
    29→}
    30→
    31→// ThemeColors defines all the colors used in the theme
    32→type ThemeColors struct {
    33→	// Primary colors
    34→	Primary   string `json:"primary"`
    35→	Secondary string `json:"secondary"`
    36→	Accent    string `json:"accent"`
    37→	Success   string `json:"success"`
    38→	Warning   string `json:"warning"`
    39→	Danger    string `json:"danger"`
    40→
    41→	// Background colors
    42→	Background      string `json:"background"`
    43→	BackgroundLight string `json:"background_light"`
    44→	BackgroundDark  string `json:"background_dark"`
    45→
    46→	// Text colors
    47→	Text      string `json:"text"`
    48→	TextMuted string `json:"text_muted"`
    49→	TextFaint string `json:"text_faint"`
    50→
    51→	// Special effects
    52→	Glow  string `json:"glow"`
    53→	Pulse string `json:"pulse"`
    54→
    55→	// Component-specific colors
    56→	Terminal struct {
    57→		Background string `json:"background"`
    58→		Foreground string `json:"foreground"`
    59→		Cursor     string `json:"cursor"`
    60→		Selection  string `json:"selection"`
    61→	} `json:"terminal"`
    62→
    63→	AI struct {
    64→		User      string `json:"user"`
    65→		Assistant string `json:"assistant"`
    66→		System    string `json:"system"`
    67→		Error     string `json:"error"`
    68→	} `json:"ai"`
    69→
    70→	System struct {
    71→		CPU     string `json:"cpu"`
    72→		Memory  string `json:"memory"`
    73→		Network string `json:"network"`
    74→		Disk    string `json:"disk"`
    75→	} `json:"system"`
    76→}
    77→
    78→// ThemeStyles defines styling preferences
    79→type ThemeStyles struct {
    80→	BorderStyle     string `json:"border_style"`     // "rounded", "normal", "thick", "double"
    81→	ProgressStyle   string `json:"progress_style"`   // "blocks", "gradient", "simple"
    82→	AnimationSpeed  string `json:"animation_speed"`  // "slow", "normal", "fast"
    83→	Transparency    bool   `json:"transparency"`     // Enable transparency effects
    84→	ShadowEffects   bool   `json:"shadow_effects"`   // Enable shadow effects
    85→	GradientEffects bool   `json:"gradient_effects"` // Enable gradient effects
    86→}
    87→
    88→// ThemeEffects defines visual effects settings
    89→type ThemeEffects struct {
    90→	EnableOrbs      bool    `json:"enable_orbs"`
    91→	EnableLightRays bool    `json:"enable_light_rays"`
    92→	EnableMatrix    bool    `json:"enable_matrix"`
    93→	EnableEnergy    bool    `json:"enable_energy"`
    94→	GlowIntensity   float64 `json:"glow_intensity"`
    95→	PulseSpeed      float64 `json:"pulse_speed"`
    96→	ParticleCount   int     `json:"particle_count"`
    97→}
    98→
    99→// NewThemeManager creates a new theme manager
   100→func NewThemeManager() *ThemeManager {
   101→	// Get config directory
   102→	configDir, err := os.UserConfigDir()
   103→	if err != nil {
   104→		configDir = "."
   105→	}
   106→	configDir = filepath.Join(configDir, "quantum-terminal")
   107→
   108→	// Create config directory if it doesn't exist
   109→	os.MkdirAll(configDir, 0755)
   110→
   111→	tm := &ThemeManager{
   112→		themes:       make(map[string]*Theme),
   113→		currentTheme: "quantum",
   114→		configDir:    configDir,
   115→	}
   116→
   117→	// Load built-in themes
   118→	tm.loadBuiltinThemes()
   119→
   120→	// Load user themes
   121→	tm.loadUserThemes()
   122→
   123→	return tm
   124→}

   832→
   833→// loadUserThemes loads themes from the user config directory
   834→func (tm *ThemeManager) loadUserThemes() {
   835→	themesDir := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes")
   836→
   837→	// Create themes directory if it doesn't exist
   838→	if err := os.MkdirAll(themesDir, 0755); err != nil {
   839→		return
   840→	}
   841→
   842→	// Read theme files
   843→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(themesDir)
   844→	if err != nil {
   845→		return
   846→	}
   847→
   848→	for _, entry := range entries {
   849→		if !entry.IsDir() && filepath.Ext(entry.Name()) == ".json" {
   850→			themePath := filepath.Join(themesDir, entry.Name())
   851→			if theme, err := tm.loadThemeFromFile(themePath); err == nil {
   852→				tm.themes[theme.Name] = theme
   853→			}
   854→		}
   855→	}
   856→}
   857→
   858→// loadThemeFromFile loads a theme from a JSON file
   859→func (tm *ThemeManager) loadThemeFromFile(filename string) (*Theme, error) {
   860→	data, err := os.ReadFile(filename)
   861→	if err != nil {
   862→		return nil, err
   863→	}
   864→
   865→	var theme Theme
   866→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &theme); err != nil {
   867→		return nil, err
   868→	}
   869→
   870→	return &theme, nil
   871→}
   872→
   873→// SaveTheme saves a theme to the user config directory
   874→func (tm *ThemeManager) SaveTheme(theme *Theme) error {
   875→	themesDir := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes")
   876→	if err := os.MkdirAll(themesDir, 0755); err != nil {
   877→		return err
   878→	}
   879→
   880→	filename := filepath.Join(themesDir, theme.Name+".json")
   881→	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(theme, "", "  ")
   882→	if err != nil {
   883→		return err
   884→	}
   885→
   886→	return os.WriteFile(filename, data, 0644)
   887→}
   888→
   889→// GetTheme returns a theme by name
   890→func (tm *ThemeManager) GetTheme(name string) (*Theme, bool) {
   891→	theme, exists := tm.themes[name]
   892→	return theme, exists
   893→}
   894→
   895→// GetCurrentTheme returns the currently active theme
   896→func (tm *ThemeManager) GetCurrentTheme() *Theme {
   897→	if theme, exists := tm.themes[tm.currentTheme]; exists {
   898→		return theme
   899→	}
   900→	// Fallback to quantum theme
   901→	return tm.themes["quantum"]
   902→}
   903→
   904→// SetCurrentTheme sets the current theme
   905→func (tm *ThemeManager) SetCurrentTheme(name string) bool {
   906→	if _, exists := tm.themes[name]; exists {
   907→		tm.currentTheme = name
   908→		return true
   909→	}
   910→	return false
   911→}
   912→
   913→// GetThemeNames returns all available theme names
   914→func (tm *ThemeManager) GetThemeNames() []string {
   915→	var names []string
   916→	for name := range tm.themes {
   917→		names = append(names, name)
   918→	}
   919→	return names
   920→}
   921→
   922→// GetThemeList returns all available themes
   923→func (tm *ThemeManager) GetThemeList() []*Theme {
   924→	var themes []*Theme
   925→	for _, theme := range tm.themes {
   926→		themes = append(themes, theme)
   927→	}
   928→	return themes
   929→}
   930→
   931→// CreateCustomTheme creates a new custom theme based on an existing theme
   932→func (tm *ThemeManager) CreateCustomTheme(baseName, newName, description string) (*Theme, error) {
   933→	baseTheme, exists := tm.themes[baseName]
   934→	if !exists {
   935→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("base theme '%s' not found", baseName)
   936→	}
   937→
   938→	// Create a copy of the base theme
   939→	newTheme := *baseTheme
   940→	newTheme.Name = newName
   941→	newTheme.Description = description
   942→	newTheme.Author = "Custom"
   943→	newTheme.Version = "1.0.0"
   944→
   945→	tm.themes[newName] = &newTheme
   946→	return &newTheme, nil
   947→}
   948→
   949→// DeleteTheme removes a user theme (built-in themes cannot be deleted)
   950→func (tm *ThemeManager) DeleteTheme(name string) error {
   951→	// Check if it's a built-in theme
   952→	builtinThemes := []string{"quantum", "cyberpunk", "matrix", "neon", "classic"}
   953→	for _, builtin := range builtinThemes {
   954→		if name == builtin {
   955→			return fmt.Errorf("cannot delete built-in theme '%s'", name)
   956→		}
   957→	}
   958→
   959→	// Remove from memory
   960→	delete(tm.themes, name)
   961→
   962→	// Remove file
   963→	filename := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes", name+".json")
   964→	if err := os.Remove(filename); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   965→		return err
   966→	}
   967→
   968→	// If it was the current theme, switch to quantum
   969→	if tm.currentTheme == name {
   970→		tm.currentTheme = "quantum"
   971→	}
   972→
   973→	return nil
   974→}
   975→
   976→// ExportTheme exports a theme to a JSON file
   977→func (tm *ThemeManager) ExportTheme(name, filename string) error {
   978→	theme, exists := tm.themes[name]
   979→	if !exists {
   980→		return fmt.Errorf("theme '%s' not found", name)
   981→	}
   982→
   983→	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(theme, "", "  ")
   984→	if err != nil {
   985→		return err
   986→	}
   987→
   988→	return os.WriteFile(filename, data, 0644)
   989→}
   990→
   991→// ImportTheme imports a theme from a JSON file
   992→func (tm *ThemeManager) ImportTheme(filename string) error {
   993→	theme, err := tm.loadThemeFromFile(filename)
   994→	if err != nil {
   995→		return err
   996→	}
   997→
   998→	tm.themes[theme.Name] = theme
   999→	return tm.SaveTheme(theme)
  1000→}
  1001→
  1002→// ApplyTheme applies a theme to the current application state
  1003→func (tm *ThemeManager) ApplyTheme(name string) error {
  1004→	if !tm.SetCurrentTheme(name) {
  1005→		return fmt.Errorf("theme '%s' not found", name)
  1006→	}
  1007→
  1008→	// Save current theme preference
  1009→	return tm.saveCurrentTheme()
  1010→}
  1011→
  1012→// saveCurrentTheme saves the current theme preference to config
  1013→func (tm *ThemeManager) saveCurrentTheme() error {
  1014→	configFile := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "current_theme")
  1015→	return os.WriteFile(configFile, []byte(tm.currentTheme), 0644)
  1016→}
  1017→
  1018→// LoadCurrentTheme loads the saved theme preference
  1019→func (tm *ThemeManager) LoadCurrentTheme() {
  1020→	configFile := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "current_theme")
  1021→	if data, err := os.ReadFile(configFile); err == nil {
  1022→		themeName := string(data)
  1023→		if _, exists := tm.themes[themeName]; exists {
  1024→			tm.currentTheme = themeName
  1025→		}
  1026→	}
  1027→}
  1028→
  1029→// GetLipglossColors converts theme colors to lipgloss colors for easy use
  1030→func (tm *ThemeManager) GetLipglossColors() map[string]lipgloss.Color {
  1031→	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
  1032→	return map[string]lipgloss.Color{
  1033→		"primary":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Primary),
  1034→		"secondary":          lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Secondary),
  1035→		"accent":             lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Accent),
  1036→		"success":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Success),
  1037→		"warning":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Warning),
  1038→		"danger":             lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Danger),
  1039→		"background":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Background),
  1040→		"background_light":   lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.BackgroundLight),
  1041→		"background_dark":    lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.BackgroundDark),
  1042→		"text":               lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Text),
  1043→		"text_muted":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.TextMuted),
  1044→		"text_faint":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.TextFaint),
  1045→		"glow":               lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Glow),
  1046→		"pulse":              lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Pulse),
  1047→		"terminal_bg":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Background),
  1048→		"terminal_fg":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Foreground),
  1049→		"terminal_cursor":    lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Cursor),
  1050→		"terminal_selection": lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Selection),
  1051→		"ai_user":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.User),
  1052→		"ai_assistant":       lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.Assistant),
  1053→		"ai_system":          lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.System),
  1054→		"ai_error":           lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.Error),
  1055→		"system_cpu":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.CPU),
  1056→		"system_memory":      lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Memory),
  1057→		"system_network":     lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Network),
  1058→		"system_disk":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Disk),
  1059→	}
  1060→}
  1061→
  1062→// GetBorderStyle returns the appropriate lipgloss border style for the theme
  1063→func (tm *ThemeManager) GetBorderStyle() lipgloss.Border {
  1064→	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
  1065→	switch theme.Styles.BorderStyle {
  1066→	case "rounded":
  1067→		return lipgloss.RoundedBorder()
  1068→	case "thick":
  1069→		return lipgloss.ThickBorder()
  1070→	case "double":
  1071→		return lipgloss.DoubleBorder()
  1072→	default:
  1073→		return lipgloss.NormalBorder()
  1074→	}
  1075→}
  1076→
  1077→// IsEffectEnabled checks if a visual effect is enabled in the current theme
  1078→func (tm *ThemeManager) IsEffectEnabled(effect string) bool {
  1079→	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
  1080→	switch effect {
  1081→	case "orbs":
  1082→		return theme.Effects.EnableOrbs
  1083→	case "light_rays":
  1084→		return theme.Effects.EnableLightRays
  1085→	case "matrix":
  1086→		return theme.Effects.EnableMatrix
  1087→	case "energy":
  1088→		return theme.Effects.EnableEnergy
  1089→	default:
  1090→		return false
  1091→	}
  1092→}
  1093→
  1094→// GetEffectSettings returns effect settings for the current theme
  1095→func (tm *ThemeManager) GetEffectSettings() ThemeEffects {
  1096→	return tm.GetCurrentTheme().Effects
  1097→}

package main

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
)

// ThemeManager handles theme loading, switching, and management
type ThemeManager struct {
	themes       map[string]*Theme
	currentTheme string
	configDir    string
}

// Theme represents a complete color theme for the application
type Theme struct {
	Name        string            `json:"name"`
	Description string            `json:"description"`
	Author      string            `json:"author"`
	Version     string            `json:"version"`
	Colors      ThemeColors       `json:"colors"`
	Styles      ThemeStyles       `json:"styles"`
	Effects     ThemeEffects      `json:"effects"`
	Custom      map[string]string `json:"custom,omitempty"`
}

// ThemeColors defines all the colors used in the theme
type ThemeColors struct {
	// Primary colors
	Primary   string `json:"primary"`
	Secondary string `json:"secondary"`
	Accent    string `json:"accent"`
	Success   string `json:"success"`
	Warning   string `json:"warning"`
	Danger    string `json:"danger"`

	// Background colors
	Background      string `json:"background"`
	BackgroundLight string `json:"background_light"`
	BackgroundDark  string `json:"background_dark"`

	// Text colors
	Text      string `json:"text"`
	TextMuted string `json:"text_muted"`
	TextFaint string `json:"text_faint"`

	// Special effects
	Glow  string `json:"glow"`
	Pulse string `json:"pulse"`

	// Component-specific colors
	Terminal struct {
		Background string `json:"background"`
		Foreground string `json:"foreground"`
		Cursor     string `json:"cursor"`
		Selection  string `json:"selection"`
	} `json:"terminal"`

	AI struct {
		User      string `json:"user"`
		Assistant string `json:"assistant"`
		System    string `json:"system"`
		Error     string `json:"error"`
	} `json:"ai"`

	System struct {
		CPU     string `json:"cpu"`
		Memory  string `json:"memory"`
		Network string `json:"network"`
		Disk    string `json:"disk"`
	} `json:"system"`
}

// ThemeStyles defines styling preferences
type ThemeStyles struct {
	BorderStyle     string `json:"border_style"`     // "rounded", "normal", "thick", "double"
	ProgressStyle   string `json:"progress_style"`   // "blocks", "gradient", "simple"
	AnimationSpeed  string `json:"animation_speed"`  // "slow", "normal", "fast"
	Transparency    bool   `json:"transparency"`     // Enable transparency effects
	ShadowEffects   bool   `json:"shadow_effects"`   // Enable shadow effects
	GradientEffects bool   `json:"gradient_effects"` // Enable gradient effects
}

// ThemeEffects defines visual effects settings
type ThemeEffects struct {
	EnableOrbs      bool    `json:"enable_orbs"`
	EnableLightRays bool    `json:"enable_light_rays"`
	EnableMatrix    bool    `json:"enable_matrix"`
	EnableEnergy    bool    `json:"enable_energy"`
	GlowIntensity   float64 `json:"glow_intensity"`
	PulseSpeed      float64 `json:"pulse_speed"`
	ParticleCount   int     `json:"particle_count"`
}

// NewThemeManager creates a new theme manager
func NewThemeManager() *ThemeManager {
	// Get config directory
	configDir, err := os.UserConfigDir()
	if err != nil {
		configDir = "."
	}
	configDir = filepath.Join(configDir, "quantum-terminal")

	// Create config directory if it doesn't exist
	os.MkdirAll(configDir, 0755)

	tm := &ThemeManager{
		themes:       make(map[string]*Theme),
		currentTheme: "quantum",
		configDir:    configDir,
	}

	// Load built-in themes
	tm.loadBuiltinThemes()

	// Load user themes
	tm.loadUserThemes()

	return tm
}

// loadBuiltinThemes loads the built-in themes

// loadUserThemes loads themes from the user config directory
func (tm *ThemeManager) loadUserThemes() {
	themesDir := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes")

	// Create themes directory if it doesn't exist
	if err := os.MkdirAll(themesDir, 0755); err != nil {
		return
	}

	// Read theme files
	entries, err := os.ReadDir(themesDir)
	if err != nil {
		return
	}

	for _, entry := range entries {
		if !entry.IsDir() && filepath.Ext(entry.Name()) == ".json" {
			themePath := filepath.Join(themesDir, entry.Name())
			if theme, err := tm.loadThemeFromFile(themePath); err == nil {
				tm.themes[theme.Name] = theme
			}
		}
	}
}

// loadThemeFromFile loads a theme from a JSON file
func (tm *ThemeManager) loadThemeFromFile(filename string) (*Theme, error) {
	data, err := os.ReadFile(filename)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	var theme Theme
	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &theme); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	return &theme, nil
}

// SaveTheme saves a theme to the user config directory
func (tm *ThemeManager) SaveTheme(theme *Theme) error {
	themesDir := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes")
	if err := os.MkdirAll(themesDir, 0755); err != nil {
		return err
	}

	filename := filepath.Join(themesDir, theme.Name+".json")
	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(theme, "", "  ")
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	return os.WriteFile(filename, data, 0644)
}

// GetTheme returns a theme by name
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetTheme(name string) (*Theme, bool) {
	theme, exists := tm.themes[name]
	return theme, exists
}

// GetCurrentTheme returns the currently active theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetCurrentTheme() *Theme {
	if theme, exists := tm.themes[tm.currentTheme]; exists {
		return theme
	}
	// Fallback to quantum theme
	return tm.themes["quantum"]
}

// SetCurrentTheme sets the current theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) SetCurrentTheme(name string) bool {
	if _, exists := tm.themes[name]; exists {
		tm.currentTheme = name
		return true
	}
	return false
}

// GetThemeNames returns all available theme names
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetThemeNames() []string {
	var names []string
	for name := range tm.themes {
		names = append(names, name)
	}
	return names
}

// GetThemeList returns all available themes
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetThemeList() []*Theme {
	var themes []*Theme
	for _, theme := range tm.themes {
		themes = append(themes, theme)
	}
	return themes
}

// CreateCustomTheme creates a new custom theme based on an existing theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) CreateCustomTheme(baseName, newName, description string) (*Theme, error) {
	baseTheme, exists := tm.themes[baseName]
	if !exists {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("base theme '%s' not found", baseName)
	}

	// Create a copy of the base theme
	newTheme := *baseTheme
	newTheme.Name = newName
	newTheme.Description = description
	newTheme.Author = "Custom"
	newTheme.Version = "1.0.0"

	tm.themes[newName] = &newTheme
	return &newTheme, nil
}

// DeleteTheme removes a user theme (built-in themes cannot be deleted)
func (tm *ThemeManager) DeleteTheme(name string) error {
	// Check if it's a built-in theme
	builtinThemes := []string{"quantum", "cyberpunk", "matrix", "neon", "classic"}
	for _, builtin := range builtinThemes {
		if name == builtin {
			return fmt.Errorf("cannot delete built-in theme '%s'", name)
		}
	}

	// Remove from memory
	delete(tm.themes, name)

	// Remove file
	filename := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "themes", name+".json")
	if err := os.Remove(filename); err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
		return err
	}

	// If it was the current theme, switch to quantum
	if tm.currentTheme == name {
		tm.currentTheme = "quantum"
	}

	return nil
}

// ExportTheme exports a theme to a JSON file
func (tm *ThemeManager) ExportTheme(name, filename string) error {
	theme, exists := tm.themes[name]
	if !exists {
		return fmt.Errorf("theme '%s' not found", name)
	}

	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(theme, "", "  ")
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	return os.WriteFile(filename, data, 0644)
}

// ImportTheme imports a theme from a JSON file
func (tm *ThemeManager) ImportTheme(filename string) error {
	theme, err := tm.loadThemeFromFile(filename)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	tm.themes[theme.Name] = theme
	return tm.SaveTheme(theme)
}

// ApplyTheme applies a theme to the current application state
func (tm *ThemeManager) ApplyTheme(name string) error {
	if !tm.SetCurrentTheme(name) {
		return fmt.Errorf("theme '%s' not found", name)
	}

	// Save current theme preference
	return tm.saveCurrentTheme()
}

// saveCurrentTheme saves the current theme preference to config
func (tm *ThemeManager) saveCurrentTheme() error {
	configFile := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "current_theme")
	return os.WriteFile(configFile, []byte(tm.currentTheme), 0644)
}

// LoadCurrentTheme loads the saved theme preference
func (tm *ThemeManager) LoadCurrentTheme() {
	configFile := filepath.Join(tm.configDir, "current_theme")
	if data, err := os.ReadFile(configFile); err == nil {
		themeName := string(data)
		if _, exists := tm.themes[themeName]; exists {
			tm.currentTheme = themeName
		}
	}
}

// GetLipglossColors converts theme colors to lipgloss colors for easy use
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetLipglossColors() map[string]lipgloss.Color {
	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
	return map[string]lipgloss.Color{
		"primary":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Primary),
		"secondary":          lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Secondary),
		"accent":             lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Accent),
		"success":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Success),
		"warning":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Warning),
		"danger":             lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Danger),
		"background":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Background),
		"background_light":   lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.BackgroundLight),
		"background_dark":    lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.BackgroundDark),
		"text":               lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Text),
		"text_muted":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.TextMuted),
		"text_faint":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.TextFaint),
		"glow":               lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Glow),
		"pulse":              lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Pulse),
		"terminal_bg":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Background),
		"terminal_fg":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Foreground),
		"terminal_cursor":    lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Cursor),
		"terminal_selection": lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.Terminal.Selection),
		"ai_user":            lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.User),
		"ai_assistant":       lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.Assistant),
		"ai_system":          lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.System),
		"ai_error":           lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.AI.Error),
		"system_cpu":         lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.CPU),
		"system_memory":      lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Memory),
		"system_network":     lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Network),
		"system_disk":        lipgloss.Color(theme.Colors.System.Disk),
	}
}

// GetBorderStyle returns the appropriate lipgloss border style for the theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetBorderStyle() lipgloss.Border {
	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
	switch theme.Styles.BorderStyle {
	case "rounded":
		return lipgloss.RoundedBorder()
	case "thick":
		return lipgloss.ThickBorder()
	case "double":
		return lipgloss.DoubleBorder()
	default:
		return lipgloss.NormalBorder()
	}
}

// IsEffectEnabled checks if a visual effect is enabled in the current theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) IsEffectEnabled(effect string) bool {
	theme := tm.GetCurrentTheme()
	switch effect {
	case "orbs":
		return theme.Effects.EnableOrbs
	case "light_rays":
		return theme.Effects.EnableLightRays
	case "matrix":
		return theme.Effects.EnableMatrix
	case "energy":
		return theme.Effects.EnableEnergy
	default:
		return false
	}
}

// GetEffectSettings returns effect settings for the current theme
func (tm *ThemeManager) GetEffectSettings() ThemeEffects {
	return tm.GetCurrentTheme().Effects
}

content.text: Now let me use Gemini to perform a comprehensive analysis of the Terminal Multitool architecture and design the Settings Page integration:

input.prompt:
Analyze the Terminal Multitool codebase architecture focusing on:

1. **Component Interface Pattern Analysis**:
   - ToolComponent interface implementation patterns
   - Message routing and state management in ViewManager
   - Component lifecycle (Init, Update, View, SetSize, SetActive)

2. **Configuration Architecture**:
   - Config struct hierarchy and validation patterns
   - ConfigManager persistence and hot reload capabilities  
   - Theme system integration with configuration

3. **Navigation and Integration Points**:
   - ViewRouter key binding system and view switching
   - Message passing between components (ConfigUpdateMsg, NotificationMsg)
   - ViewType enum and view registration patterns

Based on this analysis, design a comprehensive Settings Page component that:

1. **Follows Established Patterns**: Implements ToolComponent interface correctly
2. **Integrates Seamlessly**: Works with existing ViewManager, ConfigManager, ThemeManager
3. **Provides Rich UI**: Multi-category settings with form validation and live preview
4. **Handles Persistence**: Saves changes immediately with validation and rollback
5. **Supports Navigation**: Proper hotkey integration and navigation flow

Focus on architectural design patterns, data structures, integration points, and implementation requirements. Provide specific recommendations for:
- Settings component structure and state management
- Configuration categories and UI organization  
- Integration with existing systems (theme, config, view management)
- Navigation updates needed for settings access

content.text:
Gemini response:
Of course. Based on the file structure provided, I will analyze the existing architecture and design a new Settings Page component.

First, I need to read the key files that define the application's architecture and components.Okay, I will start by reading the files that are most relevant to the core architecture, component interfaces, and managers. This will provide the necessary context to perform the analysis and design the new settings page.Excellent. Based on the content of the provided files, here is a comprehensive architectural analysis and a design for the new Settings Page component.

### Codebase Architecture Analysis

The application is built on a well-structured, component-based architecture using the Bubble Tea (Elm) framework. The design separates concerns effectively between view management, state management, and individual UI components.

#### 1. Component Interface Pattern Analysis

*   **`ToolComponent` Interface**: This is the cornerstone of the application's modularity, defined in `types.go`. It establishes a clear contract for any "tool" or primary view in the application.
    *   `Init()`: For component-specific initialization and initial commands.
    *   `Update(msg tea.Msg)`: The core logic for handling state changes in response to messages.
    *   `View()`: Renders the component's UI using `lipgloss`.
    *   `SetSize(w, h)`: Allows the main model to inform components of terminal size changes.
    *   `SetActive(bool)`: A crucial lifecycle hook. The `ViewManager` uses this to inform a component whether it is currently visible and should be processing user input. This is key for preventing inactive components from responding to messages.

*   **Message Routing & State (`ViewManager`)**: The `ViewManager` is the central hub for components.
    *   **Registry**: It maintains a `map[ViewType]ToolComponent`, acting as a registry for all available tool views.
    *   **Lifecycle Management**: It manages the component lifecycle by calling `SetActive(true)` on the incoming view and `SetActive(false)` on the outgoing one during a `SwitchView` operation.
    *   **Message Routing**: The `RouteMessage` method is the primary router. It directs user input and system messages to the currently active component's `Update` method. It also intelligently broadcasts global messages (like `ConfigUpdateMsg`) to *all* registered components, allowing them to react to system-wide changes simultaneously.
    *   **State Preservation**: The `preserveCurrentState` and `restoreComponentState` methods are currently placeholders, indicating an architectural intent to save and restore component state on view switching, though this is not yet implemented.

#### 2. Configuration Architecture

*   **`Config` Struct Hierarchy**: The configuration, defined in `types.go`, is a well-organized set of nested structs with `yaml` tags. This cleanly separates settings by concern (e.g., `ThemeConfig`, `SystemMonitorConfig`), making the configuration file readable and the Go code type-safe.

*   **`ConfigManager` Persistence & Validation**:
    *   **Persistence**: `ConfigManager` handles loading and saving the `Config` struct to a YAML file. It uses an atomic write pattern (writing to a temporary file then renaming) to prevent config corruption.
    *   **Validation**: The validation pattern is robust. `ConfigManager` maintains a map of validator functions (`map[string]ConfigValidator`). The `ValidateConfig` method iterates through configuration fields and applies the corresponding validator, returning detailed, field-specific errors. This ensures that no invalid configuration can be saved or loaded.
    *   **Hot Reload**: The `WatchConfig` method provides a mechanism for automatic reloading. While simplified, it demonstrates the capability. In the main application loop, this would trigger a `ConfigUpdateMsg` to be broadcast to all components, ensuring the UI reflects configuration changes instantly.

*   **Theme System Integration**: The `ThemeManager` is responsible for loading and managing multiple `Theme` objects from files. The application's active theme is determined by the main `Config` struct. When a `ConfigUpdateMsg` with a new theme is received, the `ThemeManager` can be instructed to apply the new theme, and its `GetLipglossColors()` method provides the updated colors to all UI components for rendering.

#### 3. Navigation and Integration Points

*   **`ViewRouter` and Key Bindings**: The `ViewRouter` (in `view_manager.go`) is a dedicated navigation handler. It maps specific key presses (e.g., "1", "2", "!") to a `ViewType`. Upon detecting a valid key press, it creates a `ViewSwitchMsg` and dispatches it.

*   **Message Passing**: The system relies on a clear set of messages defined in `types.go`.
    *   `ViewSwitchMsg`: The primary mechanism for navigation.
    *   `ConfigUpdateMsg`: The key to achieving hot reloads and live updates. When the configuration is saved, this message is broadcast, and every component can update its state and appearance accordingly.
    *   `NotificationMsg`: A standardized way for any component to display a global notification to the user.

*   **View Registration**: The `ViewType` enum in `types.go` serves as the definitive list of all views. The `ViewManager.RegisterComponent` function links a `ViewType` to a concrete `ToolComponent` instance at startup, making the system aware of all available tools.

---

### Design: Settings Page Component

Based on the analysis, here is a comprehensive design for the `SettingsPage` component that aligns with the established architectural patterns.

#### 1. Component Structure and State Management

The `SettingsPageModel` will be a stateful struct implementing the `ToolComponent` interface.

**`settings_page.go`:**
```go
package main

import (
	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
	// ... other imports
)

type SettingsPageModel struct {
	// Core Component Requirements
	active bool
	width  int
```

### Codebase Architecture Analysis

The application is built on a well-structured, component-based architecture using the Bubble Tea (Elm) framework. The design separates concerns effectively between view management, state management, and individual UI components.

#### 1. Component Interface Pattern Analysis

*   **`ToolComponent` Interface**: This is the cornerstone of the application's modularity, defined in `types.go`. It establishes a clear contract for any "tool" or primary view in the application.
    *   `Init()`: For component-specific initialization and initial commands.
    *   `Update(msg tea.Msg)`: The core logic for handling state changes in response to messages.
    *   `View()`: Renders the component's UI using `lipgloss`.
    *   `SetSize(w, h)`: Allows the main model to inform components of terminal size changes.
    *   `SetActive(bool)`: A crucial lifecycle hook. The `ViewManager` uses this to inform a component whether it is currently visible and should be processing user input. This is key for preventing inactive components from responding to messages.

*   **Message Routing & State (`ViewManager`)**: The `ViewManager` is the central hub for components.
    *   **Registry**: It maintains a `map[ViewType]ToolComponent`, acting as a registry for all available tool views.
    *   **Lifecycle Management**: It manages the component lifecycle by calling `SetActive(true)` on the incoming view and `SetActive(false)` on the outgoing one during a `SwitchView` operation.
    *   **Message Routing**: The `RouteMessage` method is the primary router. It directs user input and system messages to the currently active component's `Update` method. It also intelligently broadcasts global messages (like `ConfigUpdateMsg`) to *all* registered components, allowing them to react to system-wide changes simultaneously.
    *   **State Preservation**: The `preserveCurrentState` and `restoreComponentState` methods are currently placeholders, indicating an architectural intent to save and restore component state on view switching, though this is not yet implemented.

#### 2. Configuration Architecture

*   **`Config` Struct Hierarchy**: The configuration, defined in `types.go`, is a well-organized set of nested structs with `yaml` tags. This cleanly separates settings by concern (e.g., `ThemeConfig`, `SystemMonitorConfig`), making the configuration file readable and the Go code type-safe.

*   **`ConfigManager` Persistence & Validation**:
    *   **Persistence**: `ConfigManager` handles loading and saving the `Config` struct to a YAML file. It uses an atomic write pattern (writing to a temporary file then renaming) to prevent config corruption.
    *   **Validation**: The validation pattern is robust. `ConfigManager` maintains a map of validator functions (`map[string]ConfigValidator`). The `ValidateConfig` method iterates through configuration fields and applies the corresponding validator, returning detailed, field-specific errors. This ensures that no invalid configuration can be saved or loaded.
    *   **Hot Reload**: The `WatchConfig` method provides a mechanism for automatic reloading. While simplified, it demonstrates the capability. In the main application loop, this would trigger a `ConfigUpdateMsg` to be broadcast to all components, ensuring the UI reflects configuration changes instantly.

*   **Theme System Integration**: The `ThemeManager` is responsible for loading and managing multiple `Theme` objects from files. The application's active theme is determined by the main `Config` struct. When a `ConfigUpdateMsg` with a new theme is received, the `ThemeManager` can be instructed to apply the new theme, and its `GetLipglossColors()` method provides the updated colors to all UI components for rendering.

#### 3. Navigation and Integration Points

*   **`ViewRouter` and Key Bindings**: The `ViewRouter` (in `view_manager.go`) is a dedicated navigation handler. It maps specific key presses (e.g., "1", "2", "!") to a `ViewType`. Upon detecting a valid key press, it creates a `ViewSwitchMsg` and dispatches it.

*   **Message Passing**: The system relies on a clear set of messages defined in `types.go`.
    *   `ViewSwitchMsg`: The primary mechanism for navigation.
    *   `ConfigUpdateMsg`: The key to achieving hot reloads and live updates. When the configuration is saved, this message is broadcast, and every component can update its state and appearance accordingly.
    *   `NotificationMsg`: A standardized way for any component to display a global notification to the user.

*   **View Registration**: The `ViewType` enum in `types.go` serves as the definitive list of all views. The `ViewManager.RegisterComponent` function links a `ViewType` to a concrete `ToolComponent` instance at startup, making the system aware of all available tools.

---

   277→		sp.editMode = false

   279→
   280→	case "enter":

   282→		sp.saveEditedValue()

   285→

   289→		}
   290→		return nil
   291→
   292→	case "right":

   759→// Actions
   760→
   761→// activateSelectedItem activates the currently selected setting item
   762→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) activateSelectedItem() tea.Cmd {
   763→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
   764→	if sp.selectedItem >= len(items) {
   765→		return nil
   766→	}
   767→
   768→	item := items[sp.selectedItem]
   769→
   770→	switch item.Type {
   771→	case ItemTypeString, ItemTypeNumber:
   772→		// Enter edit mode
   773→		sp.editMode = true
   774→		sp.editingItem = item.Name
   775→		sp.editValue = fmt.Sprintf("%v", item.Value)
   776→		sp.editCursorPos = len(sp.editValue)
   777→
   778→	case ItemTypeBool:
   779→		// Toggle boolean value
   780→		// TODO: Implement boolean toggle
   781→
   782→	case ItemTypeSelection:
   783→		// Cycle through options
   784→		// TODO: Implement selection cycling
   785→
   786→	case ItemTypeAction:
   787→		// Execute action
   788→		if item.Action != nil {
   789→			item.Action()
   790→		}
   791→
   792→	case ItemTypeColor:
   793→		// Open color picker
   794→		// TODO: Implement color picker
   795→
   796→	case ItemTypeHotkey:
   797→		// Enter hotkey capture mode
   798→		// TODO: Implement hotkey capture
   799→	}
   800→
   801→	return nil
   802→}
