   837→// Rendering
   838→
   839→// renderSettings renders the main settings interface
   840→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderSettings() string {
   841→	var s strings.Builder
   842→
   843→	// Header
   844→	header := sp.renderHeader()
   845→	s.WriteString(header + "\n")
   846→
   847→	// Navigation tabs
   848→	nav := sp.renderNavigation()
   849→	s.WriteString(nav + "\n")
   850→
   851→	// Content area
   852→	content := sp.renderContent()
   853→	s.WriteString(content)
   854→
   855→	// Status bar
   856→	status := sp.renderStatusBar()
   857→	s.WriteString(status)
   858→
   859→	return s.String()
   860→}
   861→
   862→// renderHeader renders the settings page header
   863→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderHeader() string {
   864→	title := "⚙️ QUANTUM SETTINGS"
   865→
   866→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   867→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   868→		Bold(true).
   869→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
   870→		Width(sp.width)
   871→
   872→	return titleStyle.Render(title)
   873→}
   874→
   875→// renderNavigation renders the section navigation tabs
   876→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderNavigation() string {
   877→	var tabs []string
   878→
   879→	sectionInfo := []struct {
   880→		section SettingsSection
   881→		icon    string
   882→		key     string
   883→	}{
   884→		{SectionGeneral, "⚙️", "1"},
   885→		{SectionThemes, "🎨", "2"},
   886→		{SectionHotkeys, "⌨️", "3"},
   887→		{SectionAdvanced, "🔧", "4"},
   888→		{SectionProfiles, "👤", "5"},
   889→		{SectionAbout, "ℹ️", "6"},
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	for _, info := range sectionInfo {
   893→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s %s", info.icon, info.key, info.section.String())
   894→
   895→		if info.section == sp.currentSection {
   896→			// Active tab
   897→			tabs = append(tabs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   898→				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#0f0f23")).
   899→				Background(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   900→				Bold(true).
   901→				Padding(0, 2).
   902→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   903→				BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   904→				Render(content))
   905→		} else {
   906→			// Inactive tab
   907→			tabs = append(tabs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   908→				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   909→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   910→				BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).
   911→				Padding(0, 2).
   912→				Render(content))
   913→		}
   914→	}
   915→
   916→	return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Center, tabs...)
   917→}
   918→
   919→// renderContent renders the settings content for the current section
   920→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderContent() string {
   921→	var s strings.Builder
   922→
   923→	// Section title
   924→	sectionTitle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   925→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).
   926→		Bold(true).
   927→		Render(fmt.Sprintf("📋 %s Settings", sp.currentSection.String()))
   928→	s.WriteString(sectionTitle + "\n\n")
   929→
   930→	// Settings items
   931→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
   932→	visibleHeight := sp.height - 12 // Leave space for header, nav, status
   933→
   934→	startIdx := sp.scrollOffset
   935→	endIdx := startIdx + visibleHeight
   936→	if endIdx > len(items) {
   937→		endIdx = len(items)
   938→	}
   939→
   940→	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
   941→		item := items[i]
   942→		isSelected := i == sp.selectedItem
   943→
   944→		// Render item
   945→		itemStr := sp.renderSettingsItem(item, isSelected)
   946→		s.WriteString(itemStr + "\n")
   947→	}
   948→
   949→	return s.String()
   950→}
   951→
   952→// renderSettingsItem renders a single settings item
   953→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderSettingsItem(item SettingsItem, selected bool) string {
   954→	var s strings.Builder
   955→
   956→	// Selection indicator
   957→	indicator := "  "
   958→	if selected {
   959→		indicator = "► "
   960→	}
   961→
   962→	// Item name and value
   963→	nameStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   964→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).
   965→		Bold(selected)
   966→
   967→	valueStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   968→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8"))
   969→
   970→	if selected {
   971→		nameStyle = nameStyle.Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f"))
   972→		valueStyle = valueStyle.Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f"))
   973→	}
   974→
   975→	// Format value based on type
   976→	var valueStr string
   977→	switch item.Type {
   978→	case ItemTypeBool:
   979→		if item.Value.(bool) {
   980→			valueStr = "✅ Enabled"
   981→		} else {
   982→			valueStr = "❌ Disabled"
   983→		}
   984→	case ItemTypeAction:
   985→		valueStr = "Press Enter"
   986→	default:
   987→		valueStr = fmt.Sprintf("%v", item.Value)
   988→	}
   989→
   990→	// Edit mode indicator
   991→	if sp.editMode && sp.editingItem == item.Name {
   992→		// Show edit cursor
   993→		displayValue := sp.editValue
   994→		if sp.editCursorPos < len(displayValue) {
   995→			displayValue = displayValue[:sp.editCursorPos] + "█" + displayValue[sp.editCursorPos+1:]
   996→		} else {
   997→			displayValue += "█"
   998→		}
   999→		valueStr = displayValue
  1000→		valueStyle = valueStyle.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
  1001→	}
  1002→
  1003→	itemLine := fmt.Sprintf("%s%-30s %s", indicator, item.Name, valueStr)
  1004→	s.WriteString(nameStyle.Width(sp.width - 2).Render(itemLine))
  1005→
  1006→	// Description (if selected)
  1007→	if selected && item.Description != "" {
  1008→		descStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1009→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).
  1010→			Italic(true)
  1011→		s.WriteString("\n  " + descStyle.Render(item.Description))
  1012→	}
  1013→
  1014→	return s.String()
  1015→}
  1016→
  1017→// renderStatusBar renders the settings status bar
  1018→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderStatusBar() string {
  1019→	var statusParts []string
  1020→
  1021→	// Current section info
  1022→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1023→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).Render(
  1024→			fmt.Sprintf("📂 %s", sp.currentSection.String())))
  1025→
  1026→	// Edit mode indicator
  1027→	if sp.editMode {
  1028→		statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1029→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")).Render("✏️ Editing"))
  1030→	}
  1031→
  1032→	// Item count
  1033→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
  1034→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1035→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).Render(
  1036→			fmt.Sprintf("📊 %d/%d", sp.selectedItem+1, len(items))))
  1037→
  1038→	// Help text
  1039→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1040→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981")).Render("💡 ←→: sections, ↑↓: items, Enter: edit, S: save"))
  1041→
  1042→	status := strings.Join(statusParts, " • ")
  1043→
  1044→	return lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1045→		Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f")).
  1046→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).
  1047→		Width(sp.width).
  1048→		Padding(0, 1).
  1049→		Render(status)
  1050→}
  1051→

content.text: Now let me create the comprehensive architectural analysis and design recommendations:

input.prompt:
Based on the Terminal Multitool codebase analysis, the existing SettingsPageModel implementation shows a good foundation but needs architectural improvements to properly integrate with the ConfigManager and achieve full functionality. Design comprehensive improvements for:

1. **Configuration Integration Issues**: Current settings page doesn't integrate with the ConfigManager - it only manages UI state without persisting changes
2. **Missing Validation and Error Handling**: No integration with ConfigManager's validation system
3. **Incomplete Theme Integration**: Theme changes not properly applied through ThemeManager
4. **Message Passing Gaps**: Not utilizing the established ConfigUpdateMsg/NotificationMsg system
5. **State Management Issues**: No proper backup/restore functionality for settings

Focus on architectural patterns that maintain the established component interface while adding robust configuration management, validation, persistence, and proper integration with existing systems.

content.text:
### Idea 1: Transactional State Management
**Description:** Implement a "dirty" state for settings. Changes are applied to a temporary, in-memory copy of the configuration. The `ConfigManager` only persists these changes upon receiving an explicit `SaveSettingsMsg`, and the changes can be discarded if the user cancels.
**Feasibility:** 4 | **Impact:** 5 | **Innovation:** 3
**Assessment:** This is a robust pattern for ensuring settings can be safely edited and reverted.

---
### Idea 2: Schema-Driven UI Generation
**Description:** Define the settings structure in a declarative schema (e.g., a Go struct with tags). The settings page dynamically generates UI components (text inputs, dropdowns, checkboxes) based on the schema's field types, validation rules, and descriptions.
**Feasibility:** 2 | **Impact:** 5 | **Innovation:** 5
**Assessment:** Drastically simplifies adding/removing settings and keeps the UI perfectly in sync with the config structure.

---
### Idea 3: Live Theming Preview Bus
**Description:** Create a dedicated `ThemeUpdatePreviewMsg` that the settings page emits as theme properties are changed. A `ThemeManager` service listens for these messages and applies the new theme styles in real-time without persisting them, allowing users to see changes instantly before saving.
**Feasibility:** 4 | **Impact:** 4 | **Innovation:** 4
**Assessment:** Greatly improves the user experience for theme customization.

---
### Idea 4: Asynchronous Validation Feedback Loop
**Description:** When a setting is changed, dispatch a `ValidateSettingMsg` with the key and value. The `ConfigManager` validates it asynchronously and returns either a `ValidationSuccessMsg` or a `ValidationErrorMsg` containing the field and error details, which the UI then uses to display inline error messages.
**Feasibility:** 3 | **Impact:** 5 | **Innovation:** 4
**Assessment:** Provides immediate, field-specific feedback to the user, preventing invalid configurations.

---
### Idea 5: Scoped Configuration Messages
**Description:** Instead of saving the entire configuration object at once, the UI dispatches granular messages like `UpdateConfigValueMsg{Key: "theme.color.primary", Value: "#FFFFFF"}`. This makes the system more modular and prevents unintended side effects from overwriting other settings.
**Feasibility:** 4 | **Impact:** 4 | **Innovation:** 3
**Assessment:** A clean and efficient approach to state management that scales well.

---
### Idea 6: Config Snapshot and Restore Service
**Description:** Implement methods in `ConfigManager` to `Backup()` and `Restore()` the entire application configuration to a user-specified file. The settings page would provide UI buttons to trigger these actions, showing a file picker and notifications on success or failure.
**Feasibility:** 3 | **Impact:** 4 | **Innovation:** 3
**Assessment:** Empowers users to manage and share their configurations easily.

---
### Idea 7: Centralized Notification Hub Integration
**Description:** Decouple notification logic from the settings page. On successful save or validation error, the `ConfigManager` (or the settings page) dispatches a generic `NotificationMsg`. A separate, top-level UI component is responsible for catching these messages and displaying them as toasts or status bar updates.
**Feasibility:** 5 | **Impact:** 4 | **Innovation:** 2
**Assessment:** A fundamental architectural improvement for consistent, app-wide user feedback.

---
### Idea 8: Configuration Hydration on Init
**Description:** Upon initialization, the `SettingsPageModel` sends a `RequestConfigMsg` to the `ConfigManager`. The `ConfigManager` responds with a `ConfigLoadedMsg` containing the full configuration, which the settings page then uses to populate its internal state and UI fields.
**Feasibility:** 5 | **Impact:** 5 | **Innovation:** 2
**Assessment:** The essential first step for integrating the settings page with the central configuration.

---
### Idea 9: "Reset to Defaults" Command
**Description:** Create a `ResetToDefaultsMsg`. When the `ConfigManager` receives this, it deletes the user's custom configuration file and reloads the application using the hard-coded default values. The UI would trigger this after a confirmation dialog.
**Feasibility:** 4 | **Impact:** 3 | **Innovation:** 2
**Assessment:** A standard but crucial feature for user recovery from a broken configuration.

---
### Idea 10: Hot-Reload Configuration Service
**Description:** The `ConfigManager` can be designed to watch the configuration file for external changes. If the file is modified, it automatically re-reads it, validates it, and broadcasts a `ConfigUpdatedMsg` to all components, including the settings page, ensuring the UI always reflects the persisted state.
**Feasibility:** 2 | **Impact:** 3 | **Innovation:** 4
**Assessment:** Useful for power users who edit config files directly, ensuring UI consistency.

---
### Idea 11: Context-Aware Settings Display
**Description:** The settings page model subscribes to broader application state messages. Based on the current context (e.g., which tool is active, server connection status), it can dynamically enable, disable, or hide certain settings that are not currently relevant.
**Feasibility:** 2 | **Impact:** 3 | **Innovation:** 5
**Assessment:** Creates a cleaner and more intelligent user interface by reducing irrelevant clutter.

---
### Idea 12: Pluggable Settings Modules
**Description:** Architect the settings page to be a container for "Settings Modules." Each major feature (e.g., Port Scanner, System Monitor) provides its own self-contained settings component (model and view) that registers itself with the main settings page, which then just renders them in a list.
**Feasibility:** 2 | **Impact:** 4 | **Innovation:** 4
**Assessment:** A highly scalable, decoupled architecture for managing settings in a complex, multi-tool application.

message.content:
Integrate the Settings Page into the Terminal Multitool application ecosystem.

INTEGRATION TASKS:
1. Update view_manager.go to include Settings view (View 5)
2. Add navigation hotkey '5' handling in main.go
3. Wire Settings component into the main application model
4. Update ViewType enum and related switches
5. Ensure proper message routing and state management
6. Test integration with existing views and navigation

EXAMINE:
- view_manager.go structure and patterns
- main.go navigation handling
- types.go definitions
- existing view integration patterns

Complete the full system integration ensuring seamless operation with existing functionality.

content.text: I'll integrate the Settings Page into the Terminal Multitool application ecosystem by examining the existing structure and implementing the necessary changes.

content.content:
1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"time"
     5→
     6→	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
     7→	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// ViewType represents the different tool views available in the multitool
    11→type ViewType int
    12→
    13→const (
    14→	ViewOverview ViewType = iota
    15→	ViewSystemMonitor
    16→	ViewPortScanner
    17→	ViewPackageManager
    18→	ViewContainerMgmt
    19→	ViewAIAssistant
    20→	ViewBashTerminal
    21→	ViewSettings
    22→)
    23→
    24→// String returns the string representation of ViewType
    25→func (v ViewType) String() string {
    26→	switch v {
    27→	case ViewOverview:
    28→		return "overview"
    29→	case ViewSystemMonitor:
    30→		return "system"
    31→	case ViewPortScanner:
    32→		return "scanner"
    33→	case ViewPackageManager:
    34→		return "packages"
    35→	case ViewContainerMgmt:
    36→		return "containers"
    37→	case ViewAIAssistant:
    38→		return "ai"
    39→	case ViewBashTerminal:
    40→		return "bash"
    41→	case ViewSettings:
    42→		return "settings"
    43→	default:
    44→		return "unknown"
    45→	}
    46→}
    47→
    48→// ToolComponent defines the interface that all tool components must implement
    49→type ToolComponent interface {
    50→	// Init initializes the component and returns initial commands
    51→	Init() tea.Cmd
    52→
    53→	// Update handles messages and returns updated model and commands
    54→	Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd)
    55→
    56→	// View renders the component's interface
    57→	View() string
    58→
    59→	// SetSize updates the component's dimensions
    60→	SetSize(width, height int)
    61→
    62→	// GetViewType returns the component's view type
    63→	GetViewType() ViewType
    64→
    65→	// IsActive returns whether the component is currently active
    66→	IsActive() bool
    67→
    68→	// SetActive sets the component's active state
    69→	SetActive(active bool)
    70→}
    71→
    72→// Global message types for inter-component communication
    73→type (
    74→	// tickMsg represents a tick message for animations and updates
    75→	tickMsg time.Time
    76→	// ViewSwitchMsg is sent when switching between different tool views
    77→	ViewSwitchMsg struct {
    78→		View     ViewType
    79→		Previous ViewType
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	// ConfigUpdateMsg is sent when configuration changes
    83→	ConfigUpdateMsg struct {
    84→		Config *Config
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	// NotificationMsg is sent to display notifications to the user
    88→	NotificationMsg struct {
    89→		Message string
    90→		Level   NotificationLevel
    91→		Source  string
    92→		Time    time.Time
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	// StatusUpdateMsg is sent to update the status bar
    96→	StatusUpdateMsg struct {
    97→		Status string
    98→		Color  lipgloss.Color
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	// ErrorMsg wraps errors for consistent error handling
   102→	ErrorMsg struct {
   103→		Err    error
   104→		Source string
   105→		Level  ErrorLevel
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// DataUpdateMsg is sent when shared data is updated
   109→	DataUpdateMsg struct {
   110→		Type string
   111→		Data interface{}
   112→	}
   113→)
   114→
   115→// NotificationLevel represents the severity of notifications
   116→type NotificationLevel int
   117→
   118→const (
   119→	NotificationInfo NotificationLevel = iota
   120→	NotificationWarning
   121→	NotificationError
   122→	NotificationSuccess
   123→)
   124→
   125→// String returns the string representation of NotificationLevel
   126→func (n NotificationLevel) String() string {
   127→	switch n {
   128→	case NotificationInfo:
   129→		return "info"
   130→	case NotificationWarning:
   131→		return "warning"
   132→	case NotificationError:
   133→		return "error"
   134→	case NotificationSuccess:
   135→		return "success"
   136→	default:
   137→		return "unknown"
   138→	}
   139→}
   140→
   141→// ErrorLevel represents the severity of errors
   142→type ErrorLevel int
   143→
   144→const (
   145→	ErrorLevelInfo ErrorLevel = iota
   146→	ErrorLevelWarning
   147→	ErrorLevelError
   148→	ErrorLevelCritical
   149→)
   150→
   151→// String returns the string representation of ErrorLevel
   152→func (e ErrorLevel) String() string {
   153→	switch e {
   154→	case ErrorLevelInfo:
   155→		return "info"
   156→	case ErrorLevelWarning:
   157→		return "warning"
   158→	case ErrorLevelError:
   159→		return "error"
   160→	case ErrorLevelCritical:
   161→		return "critical"
   162→	default:
   163→		return "unknown"
   164→	}
   165→}
   166→
   167→// KeyBindings holds all key binding configurations
   168→type KeyBindings struct {
   169→	Quit       []string
   170→	Help       []string
   171→	Search     []string
   172→	Kill       []string
   173→	SwitchView []string
   174→	Navigate   []string
   175→	Select     []string
   176→	Back       []string
   177→}
   178→
   179→// DefaultKeyBindings returns the default key binding configuration
   180→func DefaultKeyBindings() KeyBindings {
   181→	return KeyBindings{
   182→		Quit:       []string{"q", "ctrl+c"},
   183→		Help:       []string{"?", "h"},
   184→		Search:     []string{"/"},
   185→		Kill:       []string{"k"},
   186→		SwitchView: []string{"tab"},
   187→		Navigate:   []string{"up", "down", "j", "k"},
   188→		Select:     []string{"enter", " "},
   189→		Back:       []string{"esc"},
   190→	}
   191→}
   192→
   193→// Config holds the application configuration
   194→type Config struct {
   195→	// UI preferences
   196→	Theme        ThemeConfig       `yaml:"theme"`
   197→	KeyBindings  KeyBindings       `yaml:"keybindings"`
   198→	RefreshRates RefreshRateConfig `yaml:"refresh_rates"`
   199→
   200→	// Tool-specific settings
   201→	SystemMonitor  SystemMonitorConfig  `yaml:"system_monitor"`
   202→	PortScanner    PortScannerConfig    `yaml:"port_scanner"`
   203→	PackageManager PackageManagerConfig `yaml:"package_manager"`
   204→	ContainerMgmt  ContainerMgmtConfig  `yaml:"container_mgmt"`
   205→	AIAssistant    AIAssistantConfig    `yaml:"ai_assistant"`
   206→
   207→	// Global settings
   208→	LogLevel        string `yaml:"log_level"`
   209→	DataDirectory   string `yaml:"data_directory"`
   210→	PluginDirectory string `yaml:"plugin_directory"`
   211→}
   212→
   213→// ThemeConfig holds theme-related configuration
   214→type ThemeConfig struct {
   215→	Primary    string `yaml:"primary"`
   216→	Secondary  string `yaml:"secondary"`
   217→	Accent     string `yaml:"accent"`
   218→	Success    string `yaml:"success"`
   219→	Warning    string `yaml:"warning"`
   220→	Danger     string `yaml:"danger"`
   221→	Background string `yaml:"background"`
   222→	Text       string `yaml:"text"`
   223→}
   224→
   225→// RefreshRateConfig holds refresh rate settings for different components
   226→type RefreshRateConfig struct {
   227→	SystemStats time.Duration `yaml:"system_stats"`
   228→	Processes   time.Duration `yaml:"processes"`
   229→	Network     time.Duration `yaml:"network"`
   230→	Alerts      time.Duration `yaml:"alerts"`
   231→}
   232→
   233→// Tool-specific configuration types
   234→type SystemMonitorConfig struct {
   235→	ShowProcesses   bool          `yaml:"show_processes"`
   236→	ProcessLimit    int           `yaml:"process_limit"`
   237→	RefreshInterval time.Duration `yaml:"refresh_interval"`
   238→}
   239→
   240→type PortScannerConfig struct {
   241→	DefaultTimeout    time.Duration `yaml:"default_timeout"`
   242→	MaxConcurrency    int           `yaml:"max_concurrency"`
   243→	DefaultPortRanges []string      `yaml:"default_port_ranges"`
   244→}
   245→
   246→type PackageManagerConfig struct {
   247→	EnabledManagers []string `yaml:"enabled_managers"`
   248→	AutoUpdate      bool     `yaml:"auto_update"`
   249→	CheckSecurity   bool     `yaml:"check_security"`
   250→}
   251→
   252→type ContainerMgmtConfig struct {
   253→	DockerSocket         string `yaml:"docker_socket"`
   254→	ShowSystemContainers bool   `yaml:"show_system_containers"`
   255→}
   256→
   257→type AIAssistantConfig struct {
   258→	Provider    string  `yaml:"provider"`
   259→	APIKey      string  `yaml:"api_key"`
   260→	Model       string  `yaml:"model"`
   261→	MaxTokens   int     `yaml:"max_tokens"`
   262→	Temperature float64 `yaml:"temperature"`
   263→}
   264→
   265→// DefaultConfig returns a default configuration
   266→func DefaultConfig() *Config {
   267→	return &Config{
   268→		Theme: ThemeConfig{
   269→			Primary:    "#6366f1",
   270→			Secondary:  "#8b5cf6",
   271→			Accent:     "#06b6d4",
   272→			Success:    "#10b981",
   273→			Warning:    "#f59e0b",
   274→			Danger:     "#ef4444",
   275→			Background: "#0f0f23",
   276→			Text:       "#e2e8f0",
   277→		},
   278→		KeyBindings: DefaultKeyBindings(),
   279→		RefreshRates: RefreshRateConfig{
   280→			SystemStats: 2 * time.Second,
   281→			Processes:   2 * time.Second,
   282→			Network:     3 * time.Second,
   283→			Alerts:      5 * time.Second,
   284→		},
   285→		SystemMonitor: SystemMonitorConfig{
   286→			ShowProcesses:   true,
   287→			ProcessLimit:    100,
   288→			RefreshInterval: 2 * time.Second,
   289→		},
   290→		PortScanner: PortScannerConfig{
   291→			DefaultTimeout:    5 * time.Second,
   292→			MaxConcurrency:    100,
   293→			DefaultPortRanges: []string{"1-1000", "3000-3100", "8000-8100"},
   294→		},
   295→		PackageManager: PackageManagerConfig{
   296→			EnabledManagers: []string{"npm", "go", "pip"},
   297→			AutoUpdate:      false,
   298→			CheckSecurity:   true,
   299→		},
   300→		ContainerMgmt: ContainerMgmtConfig{
   301→			DockerSocket:         "/var/run/docker.sock",
   302→			ShowSystemContainers: false,
   303→		},
   304→		AIAssistant: AIAssistantConfig{
   305→			Provider:    "openai",
   306→			Model:       "gpt-3.5-turbo",
   307→			MaxTokens:   2000,
   308→			Temperature: 0.7,
   309→		},
   310→		LogLevel:        "info",
   311→		DataDirectory:   "~/.terminal-multitool",
   312→		PluginDirectory: "~/.terminal-multitool/plugins",
   313→	}
   314→}

    84→	secondary = lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6") // Violet

   102→var (

   108→
   109→func initialModel() model {
   110→	numCores := runtime.NumCPU()
   111→
   112→	// Initialize theme manager
   113→	themeManager := NewThemeManager()
   114→	themeManager.LoadCurrentTheme()
   115→
   116→	m := model{
   117→		viewMode:       "overview",
   118→		themeManager:   themeManager,
   119→		orbs:           make([]orb, 8),
   120→		lightRays:      make([]lightRay, 12),
   121→		matrixRain:     make([]matrixDrop, 20),
   122→		energyField:    make([]energyNode, 15),
   123→		cpuHistory:     make([][]float64, numCores),
   124→		memHistory:     make([]float64, 60),
   125→		bandwidthIn:    make([]float64, 60),
   126→		bandwidthOut:   make([]float64, 60),
   127→		networkTraffic: make([]float64, 60),
   128→		sortColumn:     "CPU",
   129→		sortReverse:    true,
   130→		selectedRow:    0,
   131→		scrollOffset:   0,
   132→		selectedPanel:  0,
   133→		glowIntensity:  0.5,
   134→		pulsePhase:     0,
   135→	}
   136→
   137→	// Initialize CPU history
   138→	for i := range m.cpuHistory {
   139→		m.cpuHistory[i] = make([]float64, 60)
   140→	}
   141→
   142→	// Initialize elegant floating orbs
   143→	colors := []lipgloss.Color{primary, secondary, accent, success, warning}
   144→	for i := range m.orbs {
   145→		m.orbs[i] = orb{
   146→			x:           rand.Float64() * 120,
   147→			y:           rand.Float64() * 40,
   148→			vx:          (rand.Float64() - 0.5) * 0.2,
   149→			vy:          (rand.Float64() - 0.5) * 0.15,
   150→			radius:      rand.Float64()*2 + 1,
   151→			intensity:   rand.Float64()*0.8 + 0.2,
   152→			color:       colors[rand.Intn(len(colors))],
   153→			pulsePeriod: rand.Float64()*4 + 2,
   154→			pulseOffset: rand.Float64() * 6.28,
   155→		}
   156→	}
   157→
   158→	// Initialize light rays
   159→	for i := range m.lightRays {
   160→		m.lightRays[i] = lightRay{
   161→			x:         rand.Float64() * 120,
   162→			y:         rand.Float64() * 40,
   163→			angle:     rand.Float64() * 6.28,
   164→			length:    rand.Float64()*15 + 5,
   165→			intensity: rand.Float64()*0.6 + 0.1,
   166→			color:     colors[rand.Intn(len(colors))],
   167→		}
   168→	}
   169→
   170→	// Initialize matrix rain
   171→	chars := []rune("ﾊﾐﾋｰｳｼﾅﾓﾆｻﾜﾂｵﾘｱﾎﾃﾏｹﾒｴｶｷﾑﾕﾗｾﾈｽﾀﾇﾍ0123456789")
   172→	for i := range m.matrixRain {
   173→		m.matrixRain[i] = matrixDrop{
   174→			x:         float64(rand.Intn(120)),
   175→			y:         rand.Float64() * 40,
   176→			speed:     rand.Float64()*0.3 + 0.1,
   177→			length:    rand.Intn(8) + 3,
   178→			chars:     make([]rune, rand.Intn(8)+3),
   179→			intensity: rand.Float64()*0.7 + 0.3,
   180→		}
   181→		for j := range m.matrixRain[i].chars {
   182→			m.matrixRain[i].chars[j] = chars[rand.Intn(len(chars))]
   183→		}
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	// Initialize global colors from theme
   187→	m.updateGlobalColors()
   188→
   189→	// Initialize energy field
   190→	for i := range m.energyField {
   191→		connections := make([]int, 0)
   192→		// Connect to 1-3 random other nodes
   193→		numConnections := rand.Intn(3) + 1
   194→		for j := 0; j < numConnections; j++ {
   195→			target := rand.Intn(len(m.energyField))
   196→			if target != i {
   197→				connections = append(connections, target)
   198→			}
   199→		}
   200→
   201→		m.energyField[i] = energyNode{
   202→			x:           rand.Float64() * 120,
   203→			y:           rand.Float64() * 40,
   204→			connections: connections,
   205→			pulsePhase:  rand.Float64() * 6.28,
   206→			energy:      rand.Float64()*0.8 + 0.2,
   207→		}
   208→	}
   209→
   210→	return m
   211→}
   212→
   213→func (m model) Init() tea.Cmd {
   214→	return tea.Batch(
   215→		tea.EnterAltScreen,
   216→		tick(),
   217→		fetchProcesses(),
   218→		fetchNetworkData(),
   219→		fetchSystemAlerts(),
   220→	)
   221→}
   222→
   223→func tick() tea.Cmd {
   224→	return tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*50, func(t time.Time) tea.Msg {
   225→		return tickMsg(t)
   226→	})
   227→}
   228→
   229→func fetchProcesses() tea.Cmd {
   230→	return func() tea.Msg {
   231→		processes, stats := getProcessesAndStats()
   232→		return processData{
   233→			processes:   processes,
   234→			systemStats: stats,
   235→			lastUpdate:  time.Now(),
   236→		}
   237→	}
   238→}
   239→
   240→type networkData struct {
   241→	connections []NetworkConnection
   242→	interfaces  []NetworkInterface
   243→	lastUpdate  time.Time
   244→}
   245→
   246→type alertData struct {
   247→	alerts     []SystemAlert
   248→	lastUpdate time.Time
   249→}
   250→
   251→func fetchNetworkData() tea.Cmd {
   252→	return func() tea.Msg {
   253→		connections := getNetworkConnections()
   254→		interfaces := getNetworkInterfaces()
   255→		return networkData{
   256→			connections: connections,
   257→			interfaces:  interfaces,
   258→			lastUpdate:  time.Now(),
   259→		}
   260→	}
   261→}
   262→
   263→func fetchSystemAlerts() tea.Cmd {
   264→	return func() tea.Msg {
   265→		alerts := getSystemAlerts()
   266→		return alertData{
   267→			alerts:     alerts,
   268→			lastUpdate: time.Now(),
   269→		}
   270→	}
   271→}
   272→
   273→func (m model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   274→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
   275→	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
   276→		m.width = msg.Width
   277→		m.height = msg.Height
   278→		return m, nil
   279→
   280→	case tea.KeyMsg:
   281→		if m.showKillDialog {
   282→			switch msg.String() {
   283→			case "y", "Y":
   284→				killProcess(m.killPID)
   285→				m.showKillDialog = false
   286→				return m, fetchProcesses()
   287→			case "n", "N", "esc":
   288→				m.showKillDialog = false
   289→				return m, nil
   290→			}
   291→			return m, nil
   292→		}
   293→
   294→		if m.searchMode {
   295→			switch msg.String() {
   296→			case "esc":
   297→				m.searchMode = false
   298→				m.searchQuery = ""
   299→				m.filteredProcs = m.processes
   300→				return m, nil
   301→			case "enter":
   302→				m.searchMode = false
   303→				return m, nil
   304→			case "backspace":
   305→				if len(m.searchQuery) > 0 {
   306→					m.searchQuery = m.searchQuery[:len(m.searchQuery)-1]
   307→					m.filteredProcs = filterProcesses(m.processes, m.searchQuery)
   308→				}
   309→				return m, nil
   310→			default:
   311→				if len(msg.String()) == 1 {
   312→					m.searchQuery += msg.String()
   313→					m.filteredProcs = filterProcesses(m.processes, m.searchQuery)
   314→				}
   315→				return m, nil
   316→			}
   317→		}
   318→
   319→		switch {
   320→		case key.Matches(msg, quitKeys):
   321→			m.quitting = true
   322→			return m, tea.Quit
   323→
   324→		case key.Matches(msg, tabKeys):
   325→			modes := []string{"overview", "processes", "network", "alerts"}
   326→			currentIndex := 0
   327→			for i, mode := range modes {
   328→				if mode == m.viewMode {
   329→					currentIndex = i
   330→					break
   331→				}
   332→			}
   333→			m.viewMode = modes[(currentIndex+1)%len(modes)]
   334→			m.selectedRow = 0
   335→			m.scrollOffset = 0
   336→			return m, nil
   337→
   338→		case msg.String() == "1":
   339→			m.viewMode = "overview"
   340→			return m, nil
   341→		case msg.String() == "2":
   342→			m.viewMode = "processes"
   343→			return m, nil
   344→		case msg.String() == "3":
   345→			m.viewMode = "network"
   346→			return m, nil
   347→		case msg.String() == "4":
   348→			m.viewMode = "alerts"
   349→			return m, nil
   350→		case msg.String() == "!":
   351→			m.viewMode = "bash"
   352→			return m, nil
   353→		case msg.String() == "t":
   354→			// Cycle through themes
   355→			return m.cycleTheme()
   356→
   357→		case key.Matches(msg, killKeys):
   358→			if m.viewMode == "processes" && len(m.filteredProcs) > 0 && m.selectedRow < len(m.filteredProcs) {
   359→				m.killPID = m.filteredProcs[m.selectedRow].PID
   360→				m.showKillDialog = true
   361→			}
   362→			return m, nil
   363→
   364→		case key.Matches(msg, searchKeys):
   365→			if m.viewMode == "processes" {
   366→				m.searchMode = true
   367→				m.searchQuery = ""
   368→			}
   369→			return m, nil
   370→
   371→		case msg.String() == "up", msg.String() == "k":
   372→			if m.selectedRow > 0 {
   373→				m.selectedRow--
   374→				if m.selectedRow < m.scrollOffset {
   375→					m.scrollOffset = m.selectedRow
   376→				}
   377→			}
   378→			return m, nil
   379→
   380→		case msg.String() == "down", msg.String() == "j":
   381→			maxItems := 0
   382→			switch m.viewMode {
   383→			case "processes":
   384→				maxItems = len(m.filteredProcs)
   385→			case "network":
   386→				maxItems = len(m.connections)
   387→			case "alerts":
   388→				maxItems = len(m.alerts)
   389→			}
   390→
   391→			if m.selectedRow < maxItems-1 {
   392→				m.selectedRow++
   393→				visibleRows := m.height - 15
   394→				if m.selectedRow >= m.scrollOffset+visibleRows {
   395→					m.scrollOffset = m.selectedRow - visibleRows + 1
   396→				}
   397→			}
   398→			return m, nil
   399→
   400→		case msg.String() == "c":
   401→			if m.viewMode == "processes" {
   402→				m.sortColumn = "CPU"
   403→				m.sortProcesses()
   404→			}
   405→			return m, nil
   406→
   407→		case msg.String() == "m":
   408→			if m.viewMode == "processes" {
   409→				m.sortColumn = "Memory"
   410→				m.sortProcesses()
   411→			}
   412→			return m, nil
   413→
   414→		case msg.String() == "p":
   415→			if m.viewMode == "processes" {
   416→				m.sortColumn = "PID"
   417→				m.sortProcesses()
   418→			}
   419→			return m, nil
   420→		}
   421→
   422→		return m, nil
   423→
   424→	case tickMsg:
   425→		m.frame++
   426→		m.pulsePhase += 0.05
   427→		m.glowIntensity = 0.3 + 0.2*math.Sin(m.pulsePhase)
   428→
   429→		// Update elegant orbs
   430→		for i := range m.orbs {
   431→			orb := &m.orbs[i]
   432→			orb.x += orb.vx
   433→			orb.y += orb.vy
   434→
   435→			// Gentle bouncing with smooth curves
   436→			if orb.x <= 0 || orb.x >= 120 {
   437→				orb.vx *= -0.8
   438→				orb.x = math.Max(0, math.Min(120, orb.x))
   439→			}
   440→			if orb.y <= 0 || orb.y >= 40 {
   441→				orb.vy *= -0.8
   442→				orb.y = math.Max(0, math.Min(40, orb.y))
   443→			}
   444→
   445→			// Update pulse
   446→			orb.intensity = 0.3 + 0.5*math.Sin(float64(m.frame)*0.02*orb.pulsePeriod+orb.pulseOffset)
   447→		}
   448→
   449→		// Update light rays
   450→		for i := range m.lightRays {
   451→			ray := &m.lightRays[i]
   452→			ray.angle += 0.01
   453→			ray.intensity = 0.2 + 0.4*math.Sin(float64(m.frame)*0.03+float64(i))
   454→		}
   455→
   456→		// Update matrix rain
   457→		for i := range m.matrixRain {
   458→			drop := &m.matrixRain[i]
   459→			drop.y += drop.speed
   460→			if drop.y > 40 {
   461→				drop.y = -float64(drop.length)
   462→				drop.x = float64(rand.Intn(120))
   463→			}
   464→		}
   465→
   466→		// Update energy field
   467→		for i := range m.energyField {
   468→			node := &m.energyField[i]
   469→			node.pulsePhase += 0.02
   470→			node.energy = 0.4 + 0.4*math.Sin(node.pulsePhase)
   471→		}
   472→
   473→		// Fetch new data periodically
   474→		var cmds []tea.Cmd
   475→		if m.frame%40 == 0 { // 2 seconds
   476→			cmds = append(cmds, fetchProcesses())
   477→		}
   478→		if m.frame%50 == 0 { // 2.5 seconds
   479→			cmds = append(cmds, fetchNetworkData())
   480→		}
   481→		if m.frame%80 == 0 { // 4 seconds
   482→			cmds = append(cmds, fetchSystemAlerts())
   483→		}
   484→
   485→		cmds = append(cmds, tick())
   486→		return m, tea.Batch(cmds...)
   487→
   488→	case processData:
   489→		m.processes = msg.processes
   490→		m.systemStats = msg.systemStats
   491→
   492→		// Update histories smoothly
   493→		for i, usage := range msg.systemStats.CPUUsage {
   494→			if i < len(m.cpuHistory) {
   495→				m.cpuHistory[i] = append(m.cpuHistory[i][1:], usage)
   496→			}
   497→		}
   498→
   499→		memUsagePercent := float64(msg.systemStats.MemUsed) / float64(msg.systemStats.MemTotal) * 100
   500→		m.memHistory = append(m.memHistory[1:], memUsagePercent)
   501→
   502→		m.sortProcesses()
   503→		m.filteredProcs = filterProcesses(m.processes, m.searchQuery)
   504→
   505→		if m.selectedRow >= len(m.filteredProcs) {
   506→			m.selectedRow = max(0, len(m.filteredProcs)-1)
   507→		}
   508→
   509→		return m, nil
   510→
   511→	case networkData:
   512→		m.connections = msg.connections
   513→		m.interfaces = msg.interfaces
   514→
   515→		// Update network traffic history
   516→		totalTraffic := 0.0
   517→		for _, iface := range m.interfaces {
   518→			totalTraffic += float64(iface.BytesIn+iface.BytesOut) / 1024 / 1024 // MB
   519→		}
   520→		m.networkTraffic = append(m.networkTraffic[1:], totalTraffic)
   521→
   522→		return m, nil
   523→
   524→	case alertData:
   525→		m.alerts = msg.alerts
   526→		return m, nil
   527→
   528→	case errMsg:
   529→		m.err = msg
   530→		return m, nil
   531→	}
   532→
   533→	return m, nil
   534→}
   535→
   536→func (m model) View() string {
   537→	if m.quitting {
   538→		return lipgloss.NewStyle().
   539→			Foreground(primary).
   540→			Align(lipgloss.Center).
   541→			Render("Disconnecting from the matrix...\n")
   542→	}
   543→
   544→	if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
   545→		return "Initializing elegant interface..."
   546→	}
   547→
   548→	// Create main layout
   549→	header := m.renderHeader()
   550→	tabs := m.renderViewTabs()
   551→	content := m.renderContent()
   552→	controls := m.renderControls()
   553→
   554→	// Add subtle background effects
   555→	bg := m.renderBackgroundEffects()
   556→
   557→	layout := lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,
   558→		header,
   559→		tabs,
   560→		content,
   561→		controls,
   562→	)
   563→
   564→	// Overlay background effects
   565→	return m.overlayEffects(layout, bg)
   566→}

   599→func (m model) renderViewTabs() string {
   600→	tabs := []struct {
   601→		key   string
   602→		name  string
   603→		icon  string
   604→		color lipgloss.Color
   605→	}{
   606→		{"1", "Overview", "⬢", primary},
   607→		{"2", "Processes", "⚡", secondary},
   608→		{"3", "Network", "◈", accent},
   609→		{"4", "Alerts", "⚠", warning},
   610→		{"!", "Bash", "⚡", success},
   611→	}
   612→
   613→	activeIndex := 0
   614→	switch m.viewMode {
   615→	case "overview":
   616→		activeIndex = 0
   617→	case "processes":
   618→		activeIndex = 1
   619→	case "network":
   620→		activeIndex = 2
   621→	case "alerts":
   622→		activeIndex = 3
   623→	case "bash":
   624→		activeIndex = 4
   625→	}
   626→
   627→	var tabStrs []string
   628→	for i, tab := range tabs {
   629→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s %s", tab.icon, tab.key, tab.name)
   630→
   631→		if i == activeIndex {
   632→			// Active tab with elegant styling
   633→			tabStrs = append(tabStrs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   634→				Foreground(bg).
   635→				Background(tab.color).
   636→				Bold(true).
   637→				Padding(0, 2).
   638→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   639→				BorderForeground(tab.color).
   640→				Render(content))
   641→		} else {
   642→			// Inactive tab with subtle styling
   643→			tabStrs = append(tabStrs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   644→				Foreground(tab.color).
   645→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   646→				BorderForeground(textFaint).
   647→				Padding(0, 2).
   648→				Render(content))
   649→		}
   650→	}
   651→
   652→	return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Center, tabStrs...) + "\n"
   653→}
   654→
   655→func (m model) renderContent() string {
   656→	switch m.viewMode {
   657→	case "overview":
   658→		return m.renderOverview()
   659→	case "processes":
   660→		return m.renderProcesses()
   661→	case "network":
   662→		return m.renderNetwork()
   663→	case "alerts":
   664→		return m.renderAlerts()
   665→	case "bash":
   666→		return m.renderBashTerminal()
   667→	default:
   668→		return m.renderOverview()
   669→	}
   670→}

   700→func (m model) renderSystemPanel() string {
   701→	var s strings.Builder
   702→
   703→	// Title with icon
   704→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   705→		Foreground(primary).
   706→		Bold(true).
   707→		Render("⬢ SYSTEM STATUS")
   708→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
   709→
   710→	// CPU information
   711→	if len(m.systemStats.CPUUsage) > 0 {
   712→		avgCPU := 0.0
   713→		for _, usage := range m.systemStats.CPUUsage {
   714→			avgCPU += usage
   715→		}
   716→		avgCPU /= float64(len(m.systemStats.CPUUsage))
   717→
   718→		cpuLabel := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render("CPU Usage:")
   719→		cpuBar := m.renderElegantBar(avgCPU, 100, 20, success, danger)
   720→		cpuText := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(textMuted).Render(fmt.Sprintf("%.1f%%", avgCPU))
   721→
   722→		s.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n%s %s\n\n", cpuLabel, cpuBar, cpuText))
   723→
   724→		// CPU cores
   725→		s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(textMuted).Render("Cores:\n"))
   726→		for i, usage := range m.systemStats.CPUUsage {
   727→			if i >= 8 {
   728→				break
   729→			} // Limit display
   730→			coreBar := m.renderMiniBar(usage, 100, 12, success, danger)
   731→			s.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("C%d %s %.1f%%\n", i, coreBar, usage))
   732→		}
   733→		s.WriteString("\n")
   734→	}
   735→
   736→	// Memory information
   737→	if m.systemStats.MemTotal > 0 {
   738→		memUsage := float64(m.systemStats.MemUsed) / float64(m.systemStats.MemTotal) * 100
   739→
   740→		memLabel := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render("Memory Usage:")
   741→		memBar := m.renderElegantBar(memUsage, 100, 20, success, danger)
   742→		memText := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(textMuted).Render(fmt.Sprintf("%.1f%% (%s/%s)",
   743→			memUsage, formatBytes(m.systemStats.MemUsed), formatBytes(m.systemStats.MemTotal)))
   744→
   745→		s.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n%s\n%s\n\n", memLabel, memBar, memText))
   746→	}
   747→
   748→	// Load average
   749→	loadText := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render("Load Average:")
   750→	loadValues := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(textMuted).Render(
   751→		fmt.Sprintf("%.2f %.2f %.2f", m.systemStats.LoadAvg[0], m.systemStats.LoadAvg[1], m.systemStats.LoadAvg[2]))
   752→	s.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n%s\n\n", loadText, loadValues))
   753→
   754→	// Uptime
   755→	uptimeText := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render("Uptime:")
   756→	uptimeValue := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(textMuted).Render(formatDuration(m.systemStats.Uptime))
   757→	s.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("%s\n%s", uptimeText, uptimeValue))
   758→
   759→	return s.String()
   760→}

   762→func (m model) renderNetworkPanel() string {
   763→	var s strings.Builder
   764→
   765→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   766→		Foreground(accent).
   767→		Bold(true).
   768→		Render("◈ NETWORK STATUS")
   769→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
   770→
   771→	// Network interfaces
   772→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render("Interfaces:\n"))
   773→	for _, iface := range m.interfaces {
   774→		if len(iface.Name) == 0 {
   775→			continue
   776→		}
   777→
   778→		ifaceStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   779→			Foreground(textMuted).
   780→			Border(lipgloss.NormalBorder(), false, false, false, true).
   781→			BorderForeground(accent).
   782→			PaddingLeft(1)
   783→
   784→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n↑ %s  ↓ %s",
   785→			iface.Name, formatBytes(iface.BytesOut), formatBytes(iface.BytesIn))
   786→
   787→		s.WriteString(ifaceStyle.Render(content) + "\n\n")
   788→	}
   789→
   790→	// Active connections
   791→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render("Active Connections:\n"))
   792→	connectionCount := min(len(m.connections), 8)
   793→	for i := 0; i < connectionCount; i++ {
   794→		conn := m.connections[i]
   795→
   796→		stateColor := success
   797→		switch conn.State {
   798→		case "ESTABLISHED":
   799→			stateColor = success
   800→		case "LISTEN":
   801→			stateColor = accent
   802→		case "TIME_WAIT":
   803→			stateColor = warning
   804→		default:
   805→			stateColor = textMuted
   806→		}
   807→
   808→		connText := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s %s:%s → %s",
   809→			conn.Protocol, conn.LocalAddr, conn.LocalPort, conn.State)
   810→		if len(connText) > 28 {
   811→			connText = connText[:25] + "..."
   812→		}
   813→
   814→		s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(stateColor).Render(connText) + "\n")
   815→	}
   816→
   817→	// Network activity visualization
   818→	s.WriteString("\n" + lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render("Activity:") + "\n")
   819→	activityViz := m.renderNetworkActivity()
   820→	s.WriteString(activityViz)
   821→
   822→	return s.String()
   823→}

   825→func (m model) renderAlertsPanel() string {
   826→	var s strings.Builder
   827→
   828→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   829→		Foreground(warning).
   830→		Bold(true).
   831→		Render("⚠ SYSTEM ALERTS")
   832→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
   833→
   834→	if len(m.alerts) == 0 {
   835→		noAlerts := lipgloss.NewStyle().

   837→			Render("✓ All systems nominal")
   838→		s.WriteString(noAlerts)
   839→		return s.String()
   840→	}
   841→
   842→	// Show recent alerts with elegant styling
   843→	maxAlerts := min(len(m.alerts), 10)
   844→	for i := 0; i < maxAlerts; i++ {
   845→		alert := m.alerts[i]
   846→
   847→		var levelColor lipgloss.Color
   848→		var icon string
   849→		switch alert.Level {
   850→		case "ERROR":
   851→			levelColor = danger
   852→			icon = "●"
   853→		case "WARN":
   854→			levelColor = warning
   855→			icon = "◐"
   856→		case "INFO":
   857→			levelColor = accent
   858→			icon = "◯"
   859→		default:
   860→			levelColor = textMuted
   861→			icon = "○"
   862→		}
   863→
   864→		timestamp := alert.Timestamp.Format("15:04")
   865→		message := alert.Message
   866→		if len(message) > 30 {
   867→			message = message[:27] + "..."
   868→		}
   869→
   870→		alertStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   871→			Foreground(levelColor).
   872→			Border(lipgloss.NormalBorder(), false, false, false, true).
   873→			BorderForeground(levelColor).
   874→			PaddingLeft(1)
   875→
   876→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s\n%s", icon, timestamp, message)
   877→		s.WriteString(alertStyle.Render(content) + "\n\n")
   878→	}
   879→
   880→	return s.String()
   881→}

   883→func (m model) renderProcesses() string {
   884→	var s strings.Builder
   885→
   886→	// Header
   887→	title := fmt.Sprintf("⚡ PROCESS MONITOR (%d processes)", len(m.filteredProcs))
   888→	if m.searchQuery != "" {
   889→		title += fmt.Sprintf(" - Filtered: '%s'", m.searchQuery)
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	headerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   893→		Foreground(secondary).
   894→		Bold(true)
   895→	s.WriteString(headerStyle.Render(title) + "\n\n")
   896→
   897→	// Column headers with elegant styling
   898→	headers := []string{"PID", "USER", "CPU%", "MEM%", "STATE", "COMMAND"}
   899→	widths := []int{8, 12, 8, 8, 8, m.width - 50}
   900→
   901→	var headerParts []string
   902→	for i, header := range headers {
   903→		headerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   904→			Foreground(primary).
   905→			Bold(true).
   906→			Width(widths[i]).
   907→			Align(lipgloss.Left)
   908→		headerParts = append(headerParts, headerStyle.Render(header))
   909→	}
   910→
   911→	s.WriteString(strings.Join(headerParts, " ") + "\n")
   912→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().
   913→		Foreground(textFaint).
   914→		Render(strings.Repeat("─", m.width-2)) + "\n\n")
   915→
   916→	// Process list
   917→	visibleRows := m.height - 15
   918→	startIdx := m.scrollOffset
   919→	endIdx := min(startIdx+visibleRows, len(m.filteredProcs))
   920→
   921→	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
   922→		if i >= len(m.filteredProcs) {
   923→			break
   924→		}
   925→
   926→		proc := m.filteredProcs[i]
   927→		isSelected := i == m.selectedRow
   928→
   929→		// Dynamic colors based on values
   930→		var cpuColor, memColor, stateColor lipgloss.Color
   931→
   932→		if proc.CPU > 80 {
   933→			cpuColor = danger
   934→		} else if proc.CPU > 40 {
   935→			cpuColor = warning
   936→		} else {
   937→			cpuColor = success
   938→		}
   939→
   940→		if proc.Memory > 10 {
   941→			memColor = danger
   942→		} else if proc.Memory > 5 {
   943→			memColor = warning
   944→		} else {
   945→			memColor = success
   946→		}
   947→
   948→		switch proc.State {
   949→		case "R", "R+":
   950→			stateColor = success
   951→		case "S", "S+":
   952→			stateColor = accent
   953→		case "T":
   954→			stateColor = warning
   955→		case "Z":
   956→			stateColor = danger
   957→		default:
   958→			stateColor = textMuted
   959→		}
   960→
   961→		// Format fields
   962→		pidText := fmt.Sprintf("%d", proc.PID)
   963→		userText := proc.User
   964→		cpuText := fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", proc.CPU)
   965→		memText := fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", proc.Memory)
   966→		stateText := proc.State
   967→
   968→		command := proc.Command
   969→		if len(command) > widths[5] {
   970→			command = command[:widths[5]-3] + "..."
   971→		}
   972→
   973→		// Create row parts
   974→		parts := []string{
   975→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[0]).Render(pidText),
   976→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[1]).Render(userText),
   977→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[2]).Foreground(cpuColor).Render(cpuText),
   978→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[3]).Foreground(memColor).Render(memText),
   979→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Width(widths[4]).Foreground(stateColor).Render(stateText),
   980→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Render(command),
   981→		}
   982→
   983→		row := strings.Join(parts, " ")
   984→
   985→		if isSelected {
   986→			row = lipgloss.NewStyle().
   987→				Foreground(bg).
   988→				Background(primary).
   989→				Bold(true).
   990→				Width(m.width - 2).
   991→				Render(" " + row + " ")
   992→		}
   993→
   994→		s.WriteString(row + "\n")
   995→	}
   996→
   997→	return s.String()
   998→}

  1000→func (m model) renderNetwork() string {
  1001→	var s strings.Builder
  1002→
  1003→	title := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1004→		Foreground(accent).
  1005→		Bold(true).
  1006→		Render("◈ NETWORK MONITOR")
  1007→	s.WriteString(title + "\n\n")
  1008→
  1009→	// Interfaces section
  1010→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Bold(true).Render("Network Interfaces:") + "\n")
  1011→	for _, iface := range m.interfaces {
  1012→		if len(iface.Name) == 0 {
  1013→			continue
  1014→		}
  1015→
  1016→		ifaceBox := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1017→			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
  1018→			BorderForeground(accent).
  1019→			Padding(1).
  1020→			Margin(0, 1, 1, 0).
  1021→			Width(30)
  1022→
  1023→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s\n\n↑ Out: %s\n↓ In:  %s\n📦 Packets: %d/%d",
  1024→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(accent).Bold(true).Render(iface.Name),
  1025→			formatBytes(iface.BytesOut),
  1026→			formatBytes(iface.BytesIn),
  1027→			iface.PacketsOut, iface.PacketsIn)
  1028→
  1029→		s.WriteString(ifaceBox.Render(content) + "\n")
  1030→	}
  1031→
  1032→	// Connections section
  1033→	s.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(text).Bold(true).Render("Active Connections:") + "\n\n")
  1034→
  1035→	// Connection headers
  1036→	connHeaders := []string{"PROTO", "LOCAL", "REMOTE", "STATE", "PROCESS"}
  1037→	connWidths := []int{8, 22, 22, 12, m.width - 70}
  1038→
  1039→	var headerParts []string
  1040→	for i, header := range connHeaders {
  1041→		headerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1042→			Foreground(accent).
  1043→			Bold(true).
  1044→			Width(connWidths[i])
  1045→		headerParts = append(headerParts, headerStyle.Render(header))
  1046→	}
  1047→
  1048→	s.WriteString(strings.Join(headerParts, " ") + "\n")
  1049→	s.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.width-2) + "\n")
  1050→
  1051→	// Connection list
  1052→	maxConns := min(len(m.connections), m.height-18)
  1053→	for i := 0; i < maxConns; i++ {
  1054→		conn := m.connections[i]
  1055→
  1056→		stateColor := success
  1057→		switch conn.State {
  1058→		case "ESTABLISHED":
  1059→			stateColor = success
  1060→		case "LISTEN":
  1061→			stateColor = accent
  1062→		case "TIME_WAIT":
  1063→			stateColor = warning
  1064→		default:
  1065→			stateColor = textMuted
  1066→		}
  1067→
  1068→		local := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", conn.LocalAddr, conn.LocalPort)
  1069→		remote := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", conn.RemoteAddr, conn.RemotePort)
  1070→
  1071→		if len(local) > connWidths[1] {
  1072→			local = local[:connWidths[1]-3] + "..."
  1073→		}
  1074→		if len(remote) > connWidths[2] {
  1075→			remote = remote[:connWidths[2]-3] + "..."
  1076→		}

  1208→// Enhanced visual effects
  1209→func (m model) renderElegantBar(value, max float64, width int, lowColor, highColor lipgloss.Color) string {
  1210→	if max == 0 {
  1211→		return ""
  1212→	}
  1213→
  1214→	ratio := value / max
  1215→	filled := int(ratio * float64(width))
  1216→	if filled > width {
  1217→		filled = width
  1218→	}
  1219→
  1220→	// Create gradient effect
  1221→	var segments []string
  1222→	for i := 0; i < width; i++ {
  1223→		if i < filled {
  1224→			if ratio > 0.7 {
  1225→				segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(highColor).Render("█"))
  1226→			} else if ratio > 0.4 {
  1227→				segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(warning).Render("█"))
  1228→			} else {
  1229→				segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lowColor).Render("█"))
  1230→			}
  1231→		} else {
  1232→			segments = append(segments, lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(textFaint).Render("░"))
  1233→		}
  1234→	}
  1235→
  1236→	return strings.Join(segments, "")
  1237→}

  1307→// Utility functions
  1308→func formatBytes(bytes uint64) string {
  1309→	const unit = 1024
  1310→	if bytes < unit {
  1311→		return fmt.Sprintf("%d B", bytes)
  1312→	}
  1313→	div, exp := int64(unit), 0
  1314→	for n := bytes / unit; n >= unit; n /= unit {
  1315→		div *= unit
  1316→		exp++
  1317→	}
  1318→	return fmt.Sprintf("%.1f %cB", float64(bytes)/float64(div), "KMGTPE"[exp])
  1319→}
  1320→
  1321→func formatDuration(d time.Duration) string {
  1322→	days := int(d.Hours()) / 24
  1323→	hours := int(d.Hours()) % 24
  1324→	minutes := int(d.Minutes()) % 60
  1325→
  1326→	if days > 0 {
  1327→		return fmt.Sprintf("%dd %dh %dm", days, hours, minutes)
  1328→	}
  1329→	if hours > 0 {
  1330→		return fmt.Sprintf("%dh %dm", hours, minutes)
  1331→	}
  1332→	return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", minutes)
  1333→}

  1339→// Process management functions
  1340→func (m *model) sortProcesses() {
  1341→	if len(m.processes) == 0 {
  1342→		return
  1343→	}
  1344→
  1345→	sort.Slice(m.processes, func(i, j int) bool {
  1346→		var less bool
  1347→		switch m.sortColumn {
  1348→		case "CPU":
  1349→			less = m.processes[i].CPU < m.processes[j].CPU
  1350→		case "Memory":
  1351→			less = m.processes[i].Memory < m.processes[j].Memory
  1352→		case "PID":
  1353→			less = m.processes[i].PID < m.processes[j].PID
  1354→		default:
  1355→			less = m.processes[i].CPU < m.processes[j].CPU
  1356→		}
  1357→		if m.sortReverse {
  1358→			less = !less
  1359→		}
  1360→		return less
  1361→	})
  1362→}

  1364→func filterProcesses(processes []Process, query string) []Process {
  1365→	if query == "" {
  1366→		return processes
  1367→	}
  1368→
  1369→	query = strings.ToLower(query)
  1370→	var filtered []Process
  1371→
  1372→	for _, proc := range processes {
  1373→		if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(proc.Name), query) ||
  1374→			strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(proc.Command), query) ||
  1375→			strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(proc.User), query) ||
  1376→			strings.Contains(fmt.Sprintf("%d", proc.PID), query) {
  1377→			filtered = append(filtered, proc)
  1378→		}
  1379→	}
  1380→
  1381→	return filtered
  1382→}

  1388→// Data fetching functions (enhanced implementations)
  1389→func getProcessesAndStats() ([]Process, SystemStats) {
  1390→	var processes []Process
  1391→	stats := SystemStats{
  1392→		CPUCores: runtime.NumCPU(),
  1393→		CPUUsage: make([]float64, runtime.NumCPU()),
  1394→	}
  1395→
  1396→	// Get CPU usage per core
  1397→	for i := 0; i < runtime.NumCPU(); i++ {
  1398→		stats.CPUUsage[i] = rand.Float64() * 100
  1399→	}
  1400→
  1401→	// Simulate memory stats
  1402→	stats.MemTotal = 8 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 // 8GB
  1403→	stats.MemUsed = uint64(rand.Float64() * float64(stats.MemTotal) * 0.8)
  1404→	stats.LoadAvg = [3]float64{rand.Float64() * 2, rand.Float64() * 2, rand.Float64() * 2}
  1405→	stats.Uptime = time.Hour * 24 * time.Duration(rand.Intn(30))
  1406→
  1407→	// Get process list via ps
  1408→	cmd := exec.Command("ps", "axo", "pid,user,pcpu,pmem,state,ppid,etime,command")
  1409→	output, err := cmd.Output()
  1410→	if err != nil {
  1411→		return processes, stats
  1412→	}
  1413→
  1414→	lines := strings.Split(string(output), "\n")
  1415→	for i, line := range lines {
  1416→		if i == 0 || line == "" {
  1417→			continue
  1418→		}
  1419→
  1420→		fields := strings.Fields(line)
  1421→		if len(fields) < 8 {
  1422→			continue
  1423→		}
  1424→
  1425→		pid, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[0])
  1426→		cpu, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[2], 64)
  1427→		mem, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(fields[3], 64)
  1428→		ppid, _ := strconv.Atoi(fields[5])
  1429→
  1430→		command := strings.Join(fields[7:], " ")
  1431→		name := fields[7]
  1432→		if strings.Contains(name, "/") {
  1433→			parts := strings.Split(name, "/")
  1434→			name = parts[len(parts)-1]
  1435→		}
  1436→
  1437→		processes = append(processes, Process{
  1438→			PID:       pid,
  1439→			Name:      name,
  1440→			User:      fields[1],
  1441→			CPU:       cpu,
  1442→			Memory:    mem,
  1443→			State:     fields[4],
  1444→			PPID:      ppid,
  1445→			StartTime: fields[6],
  1446→			Command:   command,
  1447→		})
  1448→	}
  1449→
  1450→	return processes, stats
  1451→}

  1589→// cycleTheme cycles through available themes
  1590→func (m model) cycleTheme() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
  1591→	themes := m.themeManager.GetThemeNames()
  1592→	currentTheme := m.themeManager.GetCurrentTheme().Name
  1593→
  1594→	// Find current theme index
  1595→	currentIndex := 0
  1596→	for i, theme := range themes {
  1597→		if theme == currentTheme {
  1598→			currentIndex = i
  1599→			break
  1600→		}
  1601→	}
  1602→
  1603→	// Get next theme
  1604→	nextIndex := (currentIndex + 1) % len(themes)
  1605→	nextTheme := themes[nextIndex]
  1606→
  1607→	// Apply the theme
  1608→	m.themeManager.ApplyTheme(nextTheme)
  1609→
  1610→	// Update global colors
  1611→	m.updateGlobalColors()
  1612→
  1613→	return m, nil
  1614→}

    24→
    25→	// Edit state
    26→	editMode      bool
    27→	editingItem   string
    28→	editValue     string
    29→	editCursorPos int
    30→
    31→	// Theme manager reference
    32→	themeManager *ThemeManager
    33→
    34→	// Visual effects
    35→	frame      int
    36→	pulsePhase float64
    37→}

    39→// SettingsSection represents a category of settings
    40→type SettingsSection int
    41→
    42→const (
    43→	SectionGeneral SettingsSection = iota
    44→	SectionThemes
    45→	SectionHotkeys
    46→	SectionAdvanced
    47→	SectionProfiles
    48→	SectionAbout
    49→)
    50→
    51→// String returns the string representation of SettingsSection
    52→func (s SettingsSection) String() string {
    53→	switch s {
    54→	case SectionGeneral:
    55→		return "General"
    56→	case SectionThemes:
    57→		return "Themes"
    58→	case SectionHotkeys:
    59→		return "Hotkeys"
    60→	case SectionAdvanced:
    61→		return "Advanced"
    62→	case SectionProfiles:
    63→		return "Profiles"
    64→	case SectionAbout:
    65→		return "About"
    66→	default:
    67→		return "Unknown"
    68→	}
    69→}

    97→// NewSettingsPageModel creates a new settings page component
    98→func NewSettingsPageModel(themeManager *ThemeManager) *SettingsPageModel {
    99→	sp := &SettingsPageModel{

   122→// ToolComponent interface implementation
   123→
   124→// Init initializes the settings page component
   125→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
   126→	return sp.tickCmd()
   127→}
   128→
   129→// Update handles messages for the settings page
   130→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (ToolComponent, tea.Cmd) {
   131→	var cmds []tea.Cmd
   132→
   133→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
   134→	case tea.KeyMsg:
   135→		if !sp.active {
   136→			return sp, nil
   137→		}
   138→
   139→		if cmd := sp.handleKeyMsg(msg); cmd != nil {
   140→			cmds = append(cmds, cmd)
   141→		}
   142→
   143→	case tickMsg:
   144→		sp.updateVisualEffects()
   145→		cmds = append(cmds, sp.tickCmd())
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	return sp, tea.Batch(cmds...)
   149→}

   151→// View renders the settings page interface
   152→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) View() string {
   153→	if !sp.active || sp.width == 0 || sp.height == 0 {
   154→		return ""
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	return sp.renderSettings()
   158→}

   181→// Key handling
   182→
   183→// handleKeyMsg processes key messages for the settings page
   184→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) handleKeyMsg(msg tea.KeyMsg) tea.Cmd {
   185→	if sp.editMode {
   186→		return sp.handleEditMode(msg)
   187→	}
   188→
   189→	switch msg.String() {
   190→	case "left", "h":
   191→		// Navigate to previous section
   192→		if sp.currentSection > 0 {
   193→			sp.currentSection--
   194→			sp.selectedItem = 0
   195→			sp.scrollOffset = 0
   196→		}
   197→		return nil
   198→
   199→	case "right", "l":
   200→		// Navigate to next section
   201→		if int(sp.currentSection) < len(sp.sections)-1 {
   202→			sp.currentSection++
   203→			sp.selectedItem = 0
   204→			sp.scrollOffset = 0
   205→		}
   206→		return nil
   207→
   208→	case "up", "k":
   209→		// Navigate up in current section
   210→		if sp.selectedItem > 0 {
   211→			sp.selectedItem--
   212→			if sp.selectedItem < sp.scrollOffset {
   213→				sp.scrollOffset = sp.selectedItem
   214→			}
   215→		}
   216→		return nil
   217→
   218→	case "down", "j":
   219→		// Navigate down in current section
   220→		items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
   221→		if sp.selectedItem < len(items)-1 {
   222→			sp.selectedItem++
   223→			visibleItems := sp.height - 10
   224→			if sp.selectedItem >= sp.scrollOffset+visibleItems {
   225→				sp.scrollOffset = sp.selectedItem - visibleItems + 1
   226→			}
   227→		}
   228→		return nil
   229→
   230→	case "enter", " ":
   231→		// Activate/edit selected item
   232→		return sp.activateSelectedItem()
   233→
   234→	case "esc":
   235→		// Exit edit mode or go back
   236→		if sp.editMode {
   237→			sp.editMode = false
   238→			return nil
   239→		}
   240→		// Could implement going back to main interface here
   241→		return nil
   242→
   243→	case "r":
   244→		// Reset current section to defaults
   245→		return sp.resetSectionToDefaults()
   246→
   247→	case "s":
   248→		// Save settings
   249→		return sp.saveSettings()
   250→
   251→	default:
   252→		// Handle section hotkeys
   253→		switch msg.String() {
   254→		case "1":
   255→			sp.currentSection = SectionGeneral
   256→		case "2":
   257→			sp.currentSection = SectionThemes
   258→		case "3":
   259→			sp.currentSection = SectionHotkeys
   260→		case "4":
   261→			sp.currentSection = SectionAdvanced
   262→		case "5":
   263→			sp.currentSection = SectionProfiles
   264→		case "6":
   265→			sp.currentSection = SectionAbout
   266→		}
   267→		sp.selectedItem = 0
   268→		sp.scrollOffset = 0
   269→		return nil
   270→	}
   271→}
   272→
   273→// handleEditMode handles key input while editing a setting
   274→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) handleEditMode(msg tea.KeyMsg) tea.Cmd {
   275→	switch msg.String() {
   276→	case "esc":
   277→		sp.editMode = false
   278→		return nil
   279→
   280→	case "enter":
   281→		// Save the edited value
   282→		sp.saveEditedValue()
   283→		sp.editMode = false
   284→		return nil
   285→
   286→	case "left":
   287→		if sp.editCursorPos > 0 {
   288→			sp.editCursorPos--
   289→		}
   290→		return nil
   291→
   292→	case "right":
   293→		if sp.editCursorPos < len(sp.editValue) {
   294→			sp.editCursorPos++
   295→		}
   296→		return nil
   297→
   298→	case "backspace":
   299→		if sp.editCursorPos > 0 {
   300→			sp.editValue = sp.editValue[:sp.editCursorPos-1] + sp.editValue[sp.editCursorPos:]
   301→			sp.editCursorPos--
   302→		}
   303→		return nil
   304→
   305→	case "delete":
   306→		if sp.editCursorPos < len(sp.editValue) {
   307→			sp.editValue = sp.editValue[:sp.editCursorPos] + sp.editValue[sp.editCursorPos+1:]
   308→		}
   309→		return nil
   310→
   311→	case "home", "ctrl+a":
   312→		sp.editCursorPos = 0
   313→		return nil
   314→
   315→	case "end", "ctrl+e":
   316→		sp.editCursorPos = len(sp.editValue)
   317→		return nil
   318→
   319→	default:
   320→		// Regular character input
   321→		if len(msg.String()) == 1 {
   322→			char := msg.String()
   323→			sp.editValue = sp.editValue[:sp.editCursorPos] + char + sp.editValue[sp.editCursorPos:]
   324→			sp.editCursorPos++
   325→		}
   326→		return nil
   327→	}
   328→}

   774→		sp.editingItem = item.Name

   777→

   840→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderSettings() string {
   841→	var s strings.Builder
   842→
   843→	// Header
   844→	header := sp.renderHeader()
   845→	s.WriteString(header + "\n")
   846→
   847→	// Navigation tabs
   848→	nav := sp.renderNavigation()
   849→	s.WriteString(nav + "\n")
   850→
   851→	// Content area
   852→	content := sp.renderContent()
   853→	s.WriteString(content)
   854→
   855→	// Status bar
   856→	status := sp.renderStatusBar()
   857→	s.WriteString(status)

   861→
   862→// renderHeader renders the settings page header
   863→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderHeader() string {
   864→	title := "⚙️ QUANTUM SETTINGS"
   865→
   866→	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   867→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   868→		Bold(true).
   869→		Align(lipgloss.Center).
   870→		Width(sp.width)
   871→
   872→	return titleStyle.Render(title)
   873→}
   874→
   875→// renderNavigation renders the section navigation tabs
   876→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderNavigation() string {
   877→	var tabs []string
   878→
   879→	sectionInfo := []struct {
   880→		section SettingsSection
   881→		icon    string
   882→		key     string
   883→	}{
   884→		{SectionGeneral, "⚙️", "1"},
   885→		{SectionThemes, "🎨", "2"},
   886→		{SectionHotkeys, "⌨️", "3"},
   887→		{SectionAdvanced, "🔧", "4"},
   888→		{SectionProfiles, "👤", "5"},
   889→		{SectionAbout, "ℹ️", "6"},
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	for _, info := range sectionInfo {
   893→		content := fmt.Sprintf("%s %s %s", info.icon, info.key, info.section.String())
   894→
   895→		if info.section == sp.currentSection {
   896→			// Active tab
   897→			tabs = append(tabs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   898→				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#0f0f23")).
   899→				Background(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   900→				Bold(true).
   901→				Padding(0, 2).
   902→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   903→				BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   904→				Render(content))
   905→		} else {
   906→			// Inactive tab
   907→			tabs = append(tabs, lipgloss.NewStyle().
   908→				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#6366f1")).
   909→				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
   910→				BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).
   911→				Padding(0, 2).
   912→				Render(content))
   913→		}
   914→	}
   915→
   916→	return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Center, tabs...)
   917→}
   918→
   919→// renderContent renders the settings content for the current section
   920→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderContent() string {
   921→	var s strings.Builder
   922→
   923→	// Section title
   924→	sectionTitle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   925→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).
   926→		Bold(true).
   927→		Render(fmt.Sprintf("📋 %s Settings", sp.currentSection.String()))
   928→	s.WriteString(sectionTitle + "\n\n")
   929→
   930→	// Settings items
   931→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
   932→	visibleHeight := sp.height - 12 // Leave space for header, nav, status
   933→
   934→	startIdx := sp.scrollOffset
   935→	endIdx := startIdx + visibleHeight
   936→	if endIdx > len(items) {
   937→		endIdx = len(items)
   938→	}
   939→
   940→	for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
   941→		item := items[i]
   942→		isSelected := i == sp.selectedItem
   943→
   944→		// Render item
   945→		itemStr := sp.renderSettingsItem(item, isSelected)
   946→		s.WriteString(itemStr + "\n")
   947→	}
   948→
   949→	return s.String()
   950→}
   951→
   952→// renderSettingsItem renders a single settings item
   953→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderSettingsItem(item SettingsItem, selected bool) string {
   954→	var s strings.Builder
   955→
   956→	// Selection indicator
   957→	indicator := "  "
   958→	if selected {
   959→		indicator = "► "
   960→	}
   961→
   962→	// Item name and value
   963→	nameStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   964→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).
   965→		Bold(selected)
   966→
   967→	valueStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
   968→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#94a3b8"))
   969→
   970→	if selected {
   971→		nameStyle = nameStyle.Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f"))
   972→		valueStyle = valueStyle.Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f"))
   973→	}
   974→
   975→	// Format value based on type
   976→	var valueStr string
   977→	switch item.Type {
   978→	case ItemTypeBool:
   979→		if item.Value.(bool) {
   980→			valueStr = "✅ Enabled"
   981→		} else {
   982→			valueStr = "❌ Disabled"
   983→		}
   984→	case ItemTypeAction:
   985→		valueStr = "Press Enter"
   986→	default:
   987→		valueStr = fmt.Sprintf("%v", item.Value)
   988→	}
   989→
   990→	// Edit mode indicator
   991→	if sp.editMode && sp.editingItem == item.Name {
   992→		// Show edit cursor
   993→		displayValue := sp.editValue
   994→		if sp.editCursorPos < len(displayValue) {
   995→			displayValue = displayValue[:sp.editCursorPos] + "█" + displayValue[sp.editCursorPos+1:]
   996→		} else {
   997→			displayValue += "█"
   998→		}
   999→		valueStr = displayValue
  1000→		valueStyle = valueStyle.Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b"))
  1001→	}
  1002→
  1003→	itemLine := fmt.Sprintf("%s%-30s %s", indicator, item.Name, valueStr)
  1004→	s.WriteString(nameStyle.Width(sp.width - 2).Render(itemLine))
  1005→
  1006→	// Description (if selected)
  1007→	if selected && item.Description != "" {
  1008→		descStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1009→			Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#64748b")).
  1010→			Italic(true)
  1011→		s.WriteString("\n  " + descStyle.Render(item.Description))
  1012→	}
  1013→
  1014→	return s.String()
  1015→}
  1016→
  1017→// renderStatusBar renders the settings status bar
  1018→func (sp *SettingsPageModel) renderStatusBar() string {
  1019→	var statusParts []string
  1020→
  1021→	// Current section info
  1022→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1023→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#8b5cf6")).Render(
  1024→			fmt.Sprintf("📂 %s", sp.currentSection.String())))
  1025→
  1026→	// Edit mode indicator
  1027→	if sp.editMode {
  1028→		statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1029→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#f59e0b")).Render("✏️ Editing"))
  1030→	}
  1031→
  1032→	// Item count
  1033→	items := sp.getSectionItems(sp.currentSection)
  1034→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1035→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#06b6d4")).Render(
  1036→			fmt.Sprintf("📊 %d/%d", sp.selectedItem+1, len(items))))
  1037→
  1038→	// Help text
  1039→	statusParts = append(statusParts,
  1040→		lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#10b981")).Render("💡 ←→: sections, ↑↓: items, Enter: edit, S: save"))
  1041→
  1042→	status := strings.Join(statusParts, " • ")
  1043→
  1044→	return lipgloss.NewStyle().
  1045→		Background(lipgloss.Color("#1e1e3f")).
  1046→		Foreground(lipgloss.Color("#e2e8f0")).
  1047→		Width(sp.width).
  1048→		Padding(0, 1).
  1049→		Render(status)
  1050→}
  1051→

func initialModel() model {
	numCores := runtime.NumCPU()

	// Initialize theme manager
	themeManager := NewThemeManager()
	themeManager.LoadCurrentTheme()

	// Initialize view management system
	viewRouter := NewViewRouter()
	settingsPage := NewSettingsPageModel(themeManager)

	// Register the settings page component with the view manager
	viewRouter.GetViewManager().RegisterComponent(ViewSettings, settingsPage)

	m := model{
		viewMode:       "overview",
